title: Ambiguous column reference in JOIN
fixes: Prefix column with table alias: t.column_name
Add explicit table aliases to all tables in the query
Use fully qualified names in SELECT when joining tables with same column names

regex: column.*must appear in the GROUP BY clause|not.*aggregate.*or.*GROUP BY
title: Non-aggregated column missing from GROUP BY
fixes: Add the column to GROUP BY clause
Wrap the column in an aggregate function: MAX(), MIN(), ANY_VALUE()
Remove the column from SELECT if it's not needed
Use a window function instead if per-row values are needed

title: Window function used in invalid clause
fixes: Wrap the query in a subquery/CTE and filter on the outer query
Use QUALIFY clause if your database supports it (BigQuery, Snowflake)
Move the window function to SELECT and filter in a surrounding query

title: CTE recursion limit exceeded
fixes: Add a proper termination condition to the recursive member
Set higher limit: OPTION (MAXRECURSION n) in SQL Server
Add a depth counter column and WHERE depth < max_depth
Check for cycles in the data that cause infinite recursion

title: Database deadlock between transactions
fixes: Access tables in a consistent order across transactions
Keep transactions short — minimize lock hold time
Use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE NOWAIT and retry on failure
Add retry logic with exponential backoff in application code

regex: subquery returns more than.*row|Scalar subquery produced more than one element
title: Scalar subquery returned multiple rows
fixes: Add LIMIT 1 to the subquery if only one row is expected
Use IN or EXISTS instead of = for multi-row subqueries
Add additional WHERE conditions to narrow to one row
Use an aggregate: (SELECT MAX(col) FROM ...)

title: Division by zero in SQL expression
fixes: Use NULLIF: col / NULLIF(divisor, 0)
Add CASE WHEN divisor = 0 THEN NULL ELSE col/divisor END
Filter out zero divisors in WHERE clause
Use COALESCE to provide a default: COALESCE(col / NULLIF(d, 0), 0)

title: String value exceeds column length
fixes: Increase column size: ALTER TABLE ALTER COLUMN col VARCHAR(longer)
Truncate input in application layer before insert
Use TEXT type instead of VARCHAR if length is unpredictable
Add validation to reject oversized inputs at the application level

title: ORDER BY column not in GROUP BY or aggregate
fixes: Add the ORDER BY column to GROUP BY
Wrap ORDER BY column in an aggregate function
Use a subquery: aggregate first, then sort the result

title: Query execution timeout
fixes: Add indexes on filtered and joined columns
Simplify the query — break into smaller steps with temp tables
Increase statement_timeout for legitimate long-running queries
Check EXPLAIN plan for full table scans or nested loops

regex: INSERT.*violates not-null constraint|cannot insert NULL into
title: NOT NULL constraint violation
fixes: Provide a value for the required column in the INSERT
Add a DEFAULT value to the column definition
Make the column nullable if NULL is a valid state
Set a default in the application layer before saving

title: Table or relation does not exist
fixes: Check the table name spelling and schema prefix
Run pending migrations to create the table
Verify you're connected to the correct database
Check schema search_path settings in PostgreSQL

title: LATERAL join not supported or misused
fixes: Use CROSS APPLY / OUTER APPLY in SQL Server instead
Rewrite as a correlated subquery in the SELECT clause
Use a regular JOIN with a derived table if LATERAL isn't available

title: Insufficient privileges on database object
fixes: GRANT SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE on table TO role
Check the current user: SELECT current_user
Verify schema permissions: GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA to role
Use the correct role/user in your connection string

regex: operator does not exist.*=.*|No operator matches the given name and argument type
title: Type mismatch in comparison operator
fixes: Cast one side to match: column::text = $1 or CAST(col AS type)
Check parameter types match column types in the query
Use appropriate comparison function for the data type

regex: duplicate key value violates unique constraint
title: Unique constraint violation on insert/update
fixes: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (PostgreSQL upsert)
Use MERGE or INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE (MySQL)
Check for the existing record before inserting
Review if the unique constraint is correct for your data model

title: Expensive correlated subquery or nested loop
fixes: Rewrite correlated subquery as a JOIN
Use a CTE to materialize the subquery result once
Add an index on the correlated column to speed inner lookups
Use EXISTS instead of IN for existence checks with large sets

title: Pod in CrashLoopBackOff state
fixes: Check pod logs: kubectl logs pod-name --previous
Verify the container entrypoint/command is correct
Check if liveness probe is failing and killing the container
Inspect resource limits — OOM may cause immediate restart

title: Cannot pull container image
fixes: Verify the image name and tag exist in the registry
Check imagePullSecrets is configured for private registries
Ensure the node can reach the registry (network/firewall)
Try pulling the image manually on the node: docker pull image:tag

title: Container killed due to out-of-memory
fixes: Increase memory limits in the pod spec: resources.limits.memory
Profile the application to find memory leaks
Set appropriate memory requests to ensure proper scheduling
Use JVM flags like -XX:MaxRAMPercentage for Java containers

title: Pod stuck in Pending due to insufficient resources
fixes: Scale up the cluster or add nodes with more resources
Reduce resource requests in the pod spec
Check for node taints that prevent scheduling: kubectl describe node
Remove pods with excessive resource requests from other workloads

title: Service not found or has no endpoints
fixes: Verify the service exists: kubectl get svc -n namespace
Check service selector matches pod labels exactly
Ensure pods backing the service are Running and Ready
Verify namespace — services aren't cross-namespace by default

title: ConfigMap referenced but not found
fixes: Create the ConfigMap: kubectl create configmap name --from-file=path
Check the ConfigMap name and namespace match the pod spec
Verify configMapKeyRef key exists in the ConfigMap data
Use optional: true in envFrom if ConfigMap is not always present

title: Ingress returning 404 for requests
fixes: Verify ingress rules match the request host and path
Check the backend service and port exist and have endpoints
Verify ingress controller is running: kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
Check path type: Prefix vs Exact vs ImplementationSpecific

title: TLS certificate or cert-manager failure
fixes: Check Certificate resource status: kubectl describe certificate name
Verify cert-manager is running and ClusterIssuer is configured
Check DNS records point to the correct IP for HTTP-01 validation
Inspect cert-manager logs: kubectl logs -n cert-manager deploy/cert-manager

title: Volume mount failed - PVC not found
fixes: Create the PersistentVolumeClaim before deploying the pod
Verify PVC name in pod spec matches the actual PVC name
Check StorageClass exists and can provision volumes
Ensure PVC and Pod are in the same namespace

regex: connection refused.*:6443|Unable to connect to the server
title: Cannot connect to Kubernetes API server
fixes: Check if the cluster is running: systemctl status kubelet
Verify kubeconfig points to correct server: kubectl config view
Check if VPN is required to reach the cluster
Renew certificates if expired: kubeadm certs renew all

title: Health check probe failure
fixes: Verify the probe endpoint returns 200 when the app is healthy
Increase initialDelaySeconds if the app needs more startup time
Check the probe port matches the container's listening port
Add timeoutSeconds if the health endpoint is slow

title: RBAC permission denied
fixes: Create a RoleBinding/ClusterRoleBinding for the user/service account
Check which permissions are needed: kubectl auth can-i --list
Verify the ServiceAccount is set on the pod spec
Use kubectl auth can-i verb resource --as=user to test

title: Container repeatedly restarting
fixes: Check previous container logs: kubectl logs pod --previous
Inspect exit code: kubectl describe pod name (look for Exit Code)
Verify environment variables and secrets are set correctly
Check if the container exits immediately due to wrong CMD

title: HPA unable to fetch metrics
fixes: Verify metrics-server is running: kubectl get pods -n kube-system
Check that resource requests are set on the deployment (required for CPU HPA)
Verify custom metrics adapter is configured for custom metrics
Wait a few minutes — metrics may not be available immediately after deploy

title: Network policy blocking pod communication
fixes: Check NetworkPolicy rules: kubectl get networkpolicy -n namespace
Add an ingress/egress rule allowing traffic from the source pod's labels
Verify the CNI plugin supports NetworkPolicy (not all do)
Test connectivity: kubectl exec pod -- curl target-service:port

title: Secret referenced but not found in namespace
fixes: Create the secret: kubectl create secret generic name --from-literal=key=value
Verify secret exists in the same namespace as the pod
Check secret name in pod spec for typos
Use kubectl get secrets -n namespace to list available secrets

title: Deployment rollout stuck/timed out
fixes: Check new pods: kubectl get pods -l app=name to see why they're not Ready
Increase progressDeadlineSeconds if startup is legitimately slow
Rollback: kubectl rollout undo deployment/name
Check resource quotas aren't blocking new pod creation

regex: pod has unbound immediate PersistentVolumeClaims|waiting for.*volume
title: Pod waiting for PersistentVolume to be bound
fixes: Check if a PV matching the PVC exists: kubectl get pv
Verify StorageClass provisioner is working
Check PVC accessMode and storage size match available PVs
For local volumes: ensure the node where PV is created is schedulable

title: Admission webhook rejected the request
fixes: Check the webhook error message for specific policy violations
Review the ValidatingWebhookConfiguration for the blocking webhook
Fix the resource to comply with the policy (security context, labels, etc.)
Temporarily disable the webhook if it's blocking critical fixes

title: Node at pod capacity limit
fixes: Increase max-pods in kubelet configuration
Add more nodes to the cluster
Consolidate small pods into fewer, larger containers
Check for completed/failed pods consuming slots: kubectl delete pods --field-selector=status.phase=Succeeded

regex: Error acquiring the state lock|Lock Info|lock.*already held
title: Terraform state lock cannot be acquired
fixes: Wait for the other operation to complete
Force unlock if the lock is stale: terraform force-unlock LOCK_ID
Check if a previous run crashed without releasing the lock
Verify DynamoDB table (AWS) or storage account (Azure) is accessible

regex: provider.*not found|Could not retrieve the list of available versions for provider
title: Terraform provider not found or unavailable
fixes: Run terraform init to download required providers
Add required_providers block with source in terraform {} block
Check registry connectivity (registry.terraform.io)
Use a provider mirror or filesystem_mirror for air-gapped environments

title: Resource already exists outside Terraform
fixes: Import the existing resource: terraform import resource.name id
Remove the resource from state if it shouldn't be managed: terraform state rm
Add lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true } to prevent accidental recreation
Use data source instead of resource if you just need to reference it

regex: Plan.*differ.*actual.*drift|Objects have changed outside of Terraform
title: Drift detected between state and reality
fixes: Run terraform plan to see what changed
Run terraform apply to reconcile state with config
Use terraform refresh to update state without applying
Investigate who/what made changes outside Terraform and prevent it

regex: Module not installed|Module.*not found|Could not download module
title: Terraform module not found
fixes: Run terraform init to download modules
Verify the module source path/URL is correct
Check git credentials if using private repository modules
Pin module version: source = "module?ref=v1.0.0"

title: Cloud provider credentials invalid or expired
fixes: Refresh credentials: aws sso login, az login, or gcloud auth login
Check AWS_PROFILE or environment variables are set correctly
Verify IAM role/user has required permissions
Check if temporary credentials (STS) have expired

title: Reference to undeclared Terraform object
fixes: Declare the variable in variables.tf
Check for typos in the resource reference: module.name.output
Ensure the referenced resource is in the same module or passed as variable
Run terraform validate to catch all reference errors

title: Dependency cycle in Terraform resources
fixes: Break the cycle by using depends_on with a third intermediary resource
Use data source to reference one of the resources instead
Restructure resources so dependencies flow one direction
Extract one side of the cycle into a separate module

title: Cannot destroy resource due to dependencies
fixes: Destroy dependent resources first by using target: terraform destroy -target=dependent
Remove the dependency relationship before destroying
Use force_destroy = true if the resource supports it (S3 buckets, etc.)
Empty the resource first (delete objects in bucket, detach policies)

regex: Unsupported attribute|This object does not have an attribute named
title: Accessing non-existent resource attribute
fixes: Check provider documentation for available attributes
Verify you're using the correct resource type name
Check if the attribute was renamed or removed in a provider upgrade
Use terraform console to interactively explore resource attributes

regex: value of.*cty\..*is not suitable|Inappropriate value.*string required
title: Type constraint error in variable or output
fixes: Check the variable type constraint matches the value being passed
Use tostring(), tonumber(), tolist() for explicit conversion
Verify tfvars file uses correct HCL syntax for complex types
Add explicit type in variable block: type = string

title: Backend configuration changed, init required
fixes: Run terraform init -reconfigure to reinitialize with new backend
Use terraform init -migrate-state to move state to new backend
Check that backend config values in terraform {} block are correct

title: Resource creation timed out
fixes: Increase the timeout in resource timeouts block
Check cloud provider console for the resource status
Verify network connectivity from Terraform to the cloud API
Some resources take time (RDS, EKS) — add custom timeouts { create = "60m" }

title: Invalid count or for_each argument
fixes: Ensure count value is a whole number, not a computed value that might be unknown
For for_each: use toset() to convert list to set
Cannot use for_each with values not known until apply — restructure
Move dynamic values to a local with explicit type

regex: state.*is empty|No state file found|Failed to load state
title: Terraform state file missing or empty
fixes: Check backend configuration points to correct location
Restore from state backup if available (.terraform.tfstate.backup)
Import existing resources: terraform import resource.name id
If migrating backends, use terraform init -migrate-state

title: Provider inconsistency between plan and apply
fixes: Upgrade the provider to latest version
Add ignore_changes lifecycle for the drifting attribute
Report as provider bug if consistently reproducible
Run apply again — may resolve on second attempt

title: Insufficient IAM permissions for Terraform operation
fixes: Add the required IAM policy to the Terraform execution role
Check the error for the specific action and resource ARN needed
Use AWS IAM Access Analyzer to generate minimum-privilege policy
Verify you're using the correct AWS account/profile

title: Duplicate resource definition in configuration
fixes: Remove the duplicate resource block
Use count or for_each for multiple similar resources
If imported twice, remove from state: terraform state rm one
Rename one resource and use moved block for state migration

title: Saved plan is stale due to state changes
fixes: Regenerate the plan: terraform plan -out=newplan
Ensure no other process is modifying state concurrently
Apply immediately after plan to reduce staleness window
Use -lock=true to prevent concurrent modifications

title: Provider plugin version incompatible or missing
fixes: Run terraform init -upgrade to download compatible provider version
Pin provider version in required_providers: version = "~> 4.0"
Delete .terraform directory and re-run terraform init
Check if the provider was renamed (e.g., hashicorp/aws vs aws)

title: Cannot read property of undefined in setup()
fixes: Initialize reactive refs before accessing nested properties
Add optional chaining (?.) when accessing potentially undefined objects
Use onMounted() for DOM-dependent logic instead of setup()

title: Computed property getter returns undefined
fixes: Ensure computed getter always returns a value
Check that reactive dependencies are initialized before computed runs
Add a fallback return value in the computed getter

title: Direct prop mutation warning
fixes: Use a local data/ref copy of the prop instead of mutating directly
Emit an event to let the parent update the prop value
Use v-model with update:modelValue pattern for two-way binding

regex: Property.*was accessed during render but is not defined
title: Template references undefined property
fixes: Declare the property in setup() return or data()
Check for typos in template variable names
Ensure the composable/store is properly imported and destructured

regex: v-model.*cannot be used on a prop
title: v-model used directly on prop
fixes: Create a computed with get/set that emits update event
Use defineModel() macro in Vue 3.4+
Use a local ref synced with watch on the prop

title: Reactive ref accessed without .value
fixes: Access ref value with .value in script (auto-unwrapped in template)
Check if you're passing a ref where a raw value is expected
Use unref() or toValue() when the consumer expects a plain value

title: Vue Router duplicate navigation error
fixes: Add .catch(() => {}) to router.push() calls
Check if current route matches target before navigating
Use router.replace() instead of push() for same-route updates

title: Navigation guard blocking route change
fixes: Ensure every code path in beforeEach calls next()
Return true/false or call next() — never leave guard hanging
Check async guards complete and don't throw unhandled errors

title: Pinia store used outside setup or without active instance
fixes: Call useStore() inside setup() or a composable called from setup()
Ensure createPinia() is installed on the app before using stores
For usage outside components, pass the pinia instance: useStore(pinia)

title: Pinia store action error or storeToRefs misuse
fixes: Wrap async store actions in try/catch for error handling
Pass the store instance (not destructured) to storeToRefs()
Check that the store getter doesn't throw during initialization

title: SSR hydration mismatch
fixes: Wrap browser-only code in onMounted() or <ClientOnly>
Ensure server and client render identical initial HTML
Use useId() for consistent IDs between server and client renders

title: Component resolved to undefined in template
fixes: Check component import path and ensure it's properly exported
Register the component in components option or use defineAsyncComponent()
Verify the component file has a default export

title: Infinite reactivity loop detected
fixes: Avoid modifying reactive state inside watch callbacks that watch the same state
Use watchEffect with explicit dependencies instead of deep watchers
Add a guard condition to prevent recursive updates

title: Provide/inject dependency not found
fixes: Ensure provide() is called in an ancestor component
Use a default value as second argument to inject()
Check that the injection key (Symbol or string) matches between provide and inject

title: Invalid watch source in Composition API
fixes: Pass a ref, reactive object, or getter function as watch source
Wrap plain values in () => value getter function
Don't pass .value — pass the ref itself to watch()

regex: Teleport target.*not found|Failed to locate Teleport target
title: Teleport target element missing from DOM
fixes: Ensure the target element exists before the Teleport component mounts
Use defer prop on Teleport or move target element earlier in DOM
Add the target container div to index.html outside the Vue app root

title: Compiler macros used without script setup
fixes: Use <script setup> instead of regular <script> for compiler macros
Import defineProps/defineEmits from 'vue' if not using script setup
Ensure your build tool has the Vue compiler plugin configured

title: toRef/toRefs called on non-reactive object
fixes: Pass a reactive() object to toRefs(), not a plain object or ref
Use computed() instead if you need a derived value from a ref
Wrap the object with reactive() before passing to toRefs()

regex: Suspense.*is an experimental feature|async setup.*without Suspense
title: Async component without Suspense boundary
fixes: Wrap async components with <Suspense> and provide #fallback slot
Move async logic from setup() to onMounted() if Suspense isn't needed
Use defineAsyncComponent with loading/error component options

title: ShallowRef not triggering reactivity on deep change
fixes: Call triggerRef(myRef) after mutating nested properties of a shallowRef
Replace the entire value: myRef.value = {...myRef.value, updated: true}
Use ref() instead of shallowRef() if deep reactivity is needed

regex: is not a valid SSR component
title: Component not valid for SSR rendering
fixes: Add export default to the component or check the import path
Ensure the component doesn't use browser APIs at top level
Wrap browser-only code in onMount() or check typeof window !== 'undefined'

title: Svelte $ reactive syntax error
fixes: Ensure $ reactive declarations are at the top level of a .svelte file
In Svelte 5, use $state() rune inside .svelte.ts files only with proper config
Check that the svelte preprocessor/compiler is properly configured

title: Lifecycle function called outside component initialization
fixes: Call onMount/onDestroy at the top level of the component script, not in callbacks
Move lifecycle calls out of async functions or setTimeout
Use tick() instead if you need to wait for DOM updates after state change

title: SvelteKit load function failure
fixes: Ensure load function returns an object (even empty {})
Handle fetch errors with try/catch and return error() from @sveltejs/kit
Check that server-side load functions don't use browser APIs

title: SvelteKit adapter configuration error
fixes: Install the adapter package: npm install -D @sveltejs/adapter-auto
Check svelte.config.js adapter import path matches installed package
For specific platforms, install the correct adapter (node, vercel, netlify, static)

title: Svelte store subscribe error
fixes: Ensure the store is created with writable() or readable() from svelte/store
Check that auto-subscription ($store) is used in .svelte files only
Verify the store module exports the store instance, not a factory function

title: SvelteKit API route method error
fixes: Export named functions matching HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
Check file is named +server.ts (not +page.server.ts) for API routes
Ensure the request method matches an exported handler in the endpoint file

title: Vite/SvelteKit module resolution error
fixes: Add the package to optimizeDeps.include in vite.config.ts
Check that the dependency provides ESM exports
Use ssr.noExternal for packages that need transformation during SSR

title: SvelteKit hydration mismatch
fixes: Move browser-dependent rendering into onMount() with a mounted flag
Don't use Math.random() or Date.now() during SSR — seed or defer
Use {#if browser} from $app/environment to conditionally render client-only content

title: SvelteKit form actions error
fixes: Ensure +page.server.ts exports named actions: export const actions = { default: ... }
Check that form method is POST and action attribute matches the action name
Import enhance from $app/forms and verify the action returns proper response

title: Svelte transition or animation error
fixes: Ensure elements with transitions are not inside {#each} without a key
Add a unique key to each block items: {#each items as item (item.id)}
Check that transition functions return valid CSS properties

title: Svelte slot rendering error
fixes: Define <slot> in the child component where content should render
Use named slots with slot='name' attribute on parent content
Check that the component actually accepts children/slot content

title: Svelte context API called outside initialization
fixes: Call getContext/setContext at the top level of the component script
Don't call context functions inside event handlers or async callbacks
Pass context values as props if needed in deeply nested async code

title: SvelteKit hooks.server.ts error
fixes: Ensure handle function returns a Response via resolve(event)
Check that sequence() receives valid handle functions
Verify event.locals type matches app.d.ts Locals interface

title: SvelteKit $page store access error
fixes: Import page from '$app/stores': import { page } from '$app/stores'
Access data via $page.data in .svelte files (auto-subscription)
Ensure the parent layout/page load function provides the expected data

title: Svelte binding on non-writable value
fixes: Only bind to writable variables (let declarations or writable stores)
Use on:input event handler instead of bind:value for derived values
Create a local writable copy if the source is a prop or derived store

title: SvelteKit prerender failure
fixes: Add export const prerender = false to pages that can't be prerendered
Provide entries() function in +page.ts for dynamic routes during prerender
Check that all fetch calls in load functions use absolute URLs during prerender

title: Svelte 5 runes compilation error
fixes: Ensure you're using Svelte 5+ and the file extension is .svelte or .svelte.ts
Check that runes mode is enabled in svelte.config.js if using mixed mode
Use $effect() only in component context, not in regular .ts files without runes

title: SvelteKit navigation function misuse
fixes: Use throw redirect(303, '/path') in server load functions instead of goto()
goto() is client-only — use it in event handlers or onMount, not in load()
Import redirect from @sveltejs/kit for server-side redirects

title: SvelteKit environment variable access error
fixes: Use $env/static/public for client-accessible env vars (must be prefixed PUBLIC_)
Server-only env vars from $env/static/private can only be imported in server files
Check that .env file uses correct prefix: PUBLIC_ for client, none for server-only

title: GraphQL field not found on type
fixes: Check the schema — the field may be named differently or not exist on that type
Run introspection query to verify available fields
Ensure schema has been regenerated after adding the field to resolvers

title: GraphQL variable type mismatch
fixes: Ensure variable types match the schema definition (e.g., ID! vs String!)
Check that enum values are passed as unquoted strings, not quoted
Verify nullable vs non-nullable: add ! to match schema requirements

title: GraphQL N+1 query problem
fixes: Use DataLoader to batch and cache database calls per request
Implement field-level resolvers with DataLoader instances
Add query complexity analysis to reject expensive nested queries

regex: resolver.*returned undefined|Cannot return null for non-nullable field
title: Resolver returned null for non-nullable field
fixes: Ensure resolver returns a value or change schema to nullable (remove !)
Add error handling in resolver to throw GraphQLError instead of returning null
Check database query returns data — may be a missing record issue

title: GraphQL authentication/authorization error
fixes: Pass auth token in context via the server middleware/plugin setup
Verify the context function extracts and validates the JWT/session
Check that the auth directive or guard middleware is applied to protected resolvers

title: GraphQL subscription WebSocket connection failure
fixes: Verify WebSocket server is configured (ws:// or wss:// protocol)
Check that connectionParams passes auth token for subscriptions
Ensure the PubSub engine (Redis, in-memory) is running and publishing events

regex: Schema must contain.*Query type|type Query.*not found
title: GraphQL schema missing Query type
fixes: Define at minimum a Query type in your schema: type Query { ... }
Check that schema merging/stitching includes the base Query type
Ensure typeDefs are passed as array or merged string to the server constructor

title: Duplicate type definition in schema
fixes: Use extend type Query { } in modular schemas instead of redefining Query
Check for duplicate type names across schema files
Use schema merging utilities (merge-graphql-schemas) for modular schemas

title: GraphQL fragment type condition error
fixes: Ensure fragment is defined on the correct type or interface
Use inline fragments with ... on TypeName for union type selections
Check that the type exists in schema and matches the fragment's type condition

title: GraphQL query depth/complexity limit exceeded
fixes: Simplify nested queries or split into multiple queries
Increase depth limit if legitimate use case (configure in validation rules)
Use pagination and avoid deeply nested relationship traversals

title: GraphQL mutation input validation error
fixes: Check that all required fields in the input type are provided
Verify the input object structure matches the schema's input type definition
Use a validation library in the resolver to give clear field-level errors

title: Invalid enum value in GraphQL query
fixes: Pass enum values without quotes: status: ACTIVE not status: 'ACTIVE'
Check available enum values in schema definition
Ensure client codegen is up-to-date with latest schema enums

title: Automatic Persisted Query cache miss
fixes: Client should retry with full query body when server returns 'not found'
Ensure APQ cache (Redis/in-memory) is shared across server instances
Check that the SHA256 hash generation matches between client and server

title: GraphQL union/interface missing resolveType
fixes: Add __resolveType function to union/interface resolver map
Return __typename field from the resolver for automatic type resolution
Ensure each possible type has a unique field for isTypeOf resolution

title: GraphQL file upload configuration error
fixes: Install and configure graphql-upload package for multipart request handling
Add Upload scalar to schema and configure the server middleware
Check that the client sends multipart/form-data with proper operations mapping

title: GraphQL query batching failure
fixes: Increase batch timeout configuration on the client link
Check server supports batched queries (array of operations)
Reduce batch size if payloads exceed server body size limits

title: Apollo Client cache normalization error
fixes: Ensure all types return an id or _id field for cache normalization
Configure typePolicies with keyFields for types without standard id
Use cache.evict() and cache.gc() after mutations that remove entities

title: Unknown GraphQL directive
fixes: Define custom directives in schema and implement transformer/visitor
Check directive name spelling and that it's registered with the schema
Import built-in directives (@skip, @include, @deprecated) from graphql package

title: Apollo Federation subgraph resolution error
fixes: Ensure __resolveReference is implemented for federated entity types
Check that @key directive fields are queryable in the subgraph
Verify gateway can reach all subgraph endpoints and schemas are compatible

title: GraphQL rate limiting triggered
fixes: Implement query cost analysis to assign weights to fields
Add per-client rate limiting based on token/IP in context
Use Redis-backed rate limiter shared across server instances

title: MongoDB duplicate key violation
fixes: Check for existing documents before insert, or use upsert
Verify your unique index fields don't have null values creating conflicts
Use findOneAndUpdate with upsert:true for insert-or-update patterns

title: Mongoose required field validation error
fixes: Ensure all required fields defined in schema are provided before save()
Check for typos in field names — Mongoose silently ignores unknown fields
Use validateBeforeSave: false only when intentionally skipping validation

title: Mongoose CastError invalid ObjectId
fixes: Validate the ID format before querying: mongoose.Types.ObjectId.isValid(id)
Check route params aren't receiving unexpected values (e.g., 'favicon.ico')
Use a middleware to validate ID params before they reach the handler

title: Mongoose operation buffering timed out
fixes: Ensure mongoose.connect() is called and resolves before running queries
Check MongoDB server is running and connection string is correct
Increase bufferTimeoutMS or set bufferCommands: false for fail-fast behavior

title: MongoDB transaction error
fixes: Implement retry logic for TransientTransactionError (automatic in newer drivers)
Ensure you're using a replica set — transactions require replica sets
Keep transactions short-lived to reduce write conflicts

title: MongoDB Atlas connection/authentication failure
fixes: Whitelist your IP in Atlas Network Access settings
Verify username/password are URL-encoded if they contain special characters
Check connection string uses correct cluster address and authSource=admin

title: Mongoose optimistic concurrency version error
fixes: Re-fetch the document and reapply changes before saving again
Use findOneAndUpdate() for atomic updates instead of find-modify-save
Disable versioning with { versionKey: false } if not needed

title: Mongoose populate path error
fixes: Verify the ref in schema matches the registered model name exactly
Check that the field being populated is of type ObjectId with a ref
Ensure the referenced model is registered before the populate call runs

title: MongoDB query timeout exceeded
fixes: Add indexes for the fields used in query filters and sorts
Use .explain() to identify collection scans and missing indexes
Set maxTimeMS on queries and paginate large result sets with skip/limit

title: MongoDB BSON size or type error
fixes: Documents cannot exceed 16MB — split large data into multiple documents or use GridFS
Check that field values are valid BSON types (no undefined, functions, or symbols)
Use projection to retrieve only needed fields from large documents

title: Mongoose model not registered
fixes: Register the model with mongoose.model('Name', schema) before referencing it
Check circular imports — model file may not have executed before reference
Ensure model registration runs at app startup, not lazily inside functions

title: Mongoose model compiled multiple times
fixes: Use mongoose.models.Name || mongoose.model('Name', schema) pattern
In Next.js/serverless, check if model exists before redefining
Move model definitions to their own module imported once at startup

title: MongoDB unknown query operator
fixes: Check operator spelling ($gt, $gte, $in, $regex, etc.)
Ensure filter objects aren't accidentally nested: {field: {$gt: 5}} not {field: {gt: 5}}
Verify you're not passing a Mongoose query object where a plain filter is expected

title: MongoDB conflicting index definition
fixes: Drop the existing index before creating with new options: collection.dropIndex()
Use the same index name or let MongoDB auto-generate it
Check that unique, sparse, and TTL options match the existing index

title: MongoDB connection pool destroyed
fixes: Don't call mongoose.disconnect() while operations are pending
Handle SIGINT/SIGTERM gracefully — close connections after pending ops complete
Check for connection event listeners and reconnect logic in your app

title: MongoDB Change Stream error
fixes: Store the resume token and use it to resume the stream after disconnection
Ensure oplog has sufficient size for your change stream throughput
Use a replica set or sharded cluster — change streams require them

title: MongoDB aggregation pipeline error
fixes: Check stage order — $match before $group for performance, $project field names
Verify $lookup foreign collection and field names match exactly
Use .explain() to debug pipeline stages and ensure proper index usage

title: Mongoose discriminator error
fixes: Define the base schema first, then add discriminators on the base model
Ensure discriminator key field is not redefined in child schema
Use the base model for queries that span all discriminated types

title: MongoDB TLS/SSL connection error
fixes: Add tls=true&tlsCAFile=path/to/ca.pem to connection string for Atlas
For self-signed certs in dev, use tlsAllowInvalidCertificates=true
Ensure Node.js trusts the CA — set NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS env variable

title: MongoDB cursor already closed/exhausted
fixes: Don't reuse cursors — create a new query/find() for each iteration
Increase cursor timeout with noCursorTimeout option for long-running reads
Use cursor.hasNext() before calling cursor.next() to avoid exhaustion

regex: WRONGTYPE Operation against a key holding the wrong kind of value
title: Redis WRONGTYPE operation on wrong data structure
fixes: Check key's type with TYPE command before operating on it
Delete the key and recreate with correct type if data structure changed
Use different key names for different data types (e.g., user:1:name vs user:1:sessions)

title: Redis connection refused
fixes: Verify Redis server is running: redis-cli ping
Check host and port in connection config (default: localhost:6379)
Ensure firewall/security group allows connections on Redis port

title: Redis max memory limit reached
fixes: Configure an eviction policy: maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
Increase maxmemory in redis.conf or use CONFIG SET
Audit key usage with redis-cli --bigkeys and remove/expire unnecessary data

title: Redis serialization error
fixes: Serialize complex objects with JSON.stringify() before storing
Check for circular references in objects before caching
Use a serialization library (msgpack, protobuf) for non-JSON-safe values

title: Redis pub/sub subscriber mode command error
fixes: Use separate Redis client instances for pub/sub and regular commands
The subscriber client can only use SUBSCRIBE/UNSUBSCRIBE/PSUBSCRIBE after subscribing
Create a dedicated connection for subscriptions, another for get/set operations

title: Redis authentication failure
fixes: Pass password in connection config: { password: 'your-password' }
For Redis 6+ ACLs, provide both username and password
Check requirepass in redis.conf matches your client configuration

title: Redis Cluster cross-slot key error
fixes: Use hash tags {prefix} in key names to ensure related keys map to same slot
Don't use multi-key commands (MGET, MSET) across different slots
Use a cluster-aware client that handles MOVED/ASK redirections automatically

title: Redis transaction (MULTI/EXEC) error
fixes: Check all commands in the transaction are valid before EXEC
Use WATCH for optimistic locking — transaction aborts if watched key changes
Handle null EXEC response (transaction aborted) with retry logic

title: Redis server busy loading data
fixes: Wait for Redis to finish loading the RDB/AOF file before connecting
Implement connection retry with exponential backoff on LOADING error
Reduce dataset size or use faster storage to speed up Redis startup

title: Redis Lua script timeout
fixes: Optimize Lua scripts to avoid long-running operations
Increase lua-time-limit config if the script legitimately needs more time
Use SCRIPT KILL to terminate the script (only works if no writes occurred)

title: Redis Streams consumer group error
fixes: Create the stream and consumer group before reading: XGROUP CREATE mystream grp $ MKSTREAM
Handle NOGROUP error by auto-creating the group on first use
Use XINFO GROUPS to verify consumer group exists and check pending entries

title: Redis client connection closed unexpectedly
fixes: Enable auto-reconnect in client config (reconnectStrategy in ioredis/node-redis)
Handle 'error' and 'reconnecting' events on the client for graceful degradation
Check Redis server logs for OOM kills, max client limits, or timeout disconnects

title: Redis background save failure
fixes: Check disk space — Redis needs free space for RDB snapshot writes
Set stop-writes-on-bgsave-error no if persistence isn't critical
Fix file system permissions on the Redis data directory (dump.rdb location)

title: Redis max clients limit reached
fixes: Increase maxclients in redis.conf (default 10000)
Use connection pooling in your application (e.g., generic-pool)
Audit and close idle connections — check CLIENT LIST for leaked connections

title: Redis invalid TTL/expire value
fixes: Ensure TTL is a positive integer (seconds for EXPIRE, milliseconds for PEXPIRE)
Check that the value isn't NaN or undefined before passing to expire command
Use EX (seconds) or PX (milliseconds) option with SET for atomic set+expire

title: Redis EVALSHA script not in cache
fixes: Fall back to EVAL with full script when EVALSHA returns NOSCRIPT
Use SCRIPT LOAD on application startup to pre-cache scripts
After Redis restart, scripts must be reloaded — implement automatic reload logic

title: Redis pub/sub message size issue
fixes: Keep pub/sub messages small — send IDs/references, not full payloads
Compress large messages before publishing (gzip/lz4)
Store large data in a key and publish just the key name via pub/sub

title: Redis write to replica/slave error
fixes: Direct write operations to the master node, not replicas
Use a cluster-aware client that routes writes to master automatically
Check if a failover occurred — the old master may now be a replica

title: Redis SCAN cursor error
fixes: Always start SCAN with cursor 0 and continue until server returns 0
Don't reuse cursors from previous SCAN iterations after reconnection
Handle cursor as a string (not number) — some clients return BigInt for large cursors

title: Redis Sentinel master discovery failure
fixes: Verify sentinel nodes are running and can reach the Redis master
Check master name in client config matches sentinel's monitored master name
Ensure at least quorum number of sentinels agree on master location

title: Webpack/Vite module not found
fixes: Install the missing package: npm install <package-name>
Check import path for typos and correct relative path resolution
Add resolve.alias in webpack/vite config for custom path mappings

regex: You may need an appropriate loader|Module parse failed.*Unexpected token
title: Webpack missing loader for file type
fixes: Install and configure the appropriate loader (babel-loader, ts-loader, css-loader)
Add the file extension to module.rules in webpack config
Check that the loader rule test regex matches the file extension

title: Dynamic chunk loading failure at runtime
fixes: Add retry logic for dynamic imports: catch error and retry import()
Ensure CDN/server serves all chunk files from correct publicPath
Check for filename hash changes after deploy — users may have stale HTML

title: HMR (Hot Module Replacement) failure
fixes: Full page reload usually recovers — check for syntax errors in changed file
Ensure module.hot.accept() is configured for custom HMR boundaries
Check WebSocket connection to dev server isn't blocked by proxy/firewall

title: Circular dependency warning
fixes: Refactor shared code into a separate module imported by both sides
Use dependency injection or lazy imports to break the cycle
Run circular-dependency-plugin to identify all cycles and prioritize fixes

title: Tree-shaking removing needed code
fixes: Mark files with side effects in package.json sideEffects array
Use /*#__PURE__*/ annotation for tree-shakable function calls
Check that imports use named exports, not namespace imports (import * as)

title: CSS extraction ordering conflict
fixes: Ensure consistent import order of CSS modules across entry points
Set ignoreOrder: true in MiniCssExtractPlugin options (if order doesn't matter)
Consolidate CSS imports into a single entry point to guarantee order

title: Node.js process variable in browser bundle
fixes: Use import.meta.env instead of process.env in Vite projects
Add define: { 'process.env': {} } to vite.config.ts for compatibility
For webpack, configure DefinePlugin to replace process.env at build time

regex: Cannot use import statement outside a module|SyntaxError.*import.*ESM
title: ESM/CJS module interop error
fixes: Add "type": "module" to package.json or use .mjs extension
Configure babel/ts to output correct module format for your bundler
Use dynamic import() for ESM modules in CJS context

title: PostCSS plugin processing error
fixes: Check PostCSS config file (postcss.config.js) for correct plugin format
Install missing PostCSS plugins: npm install autoprefixer postcss
Ensure CSS syntax is valid — PostCSS fails on certain invalid selectors

title: Vite dependency pre-bundling error
fixes: Delete node_modules/.vite and restart dev server
Add problematic package to optimizeDeps.include in vite.config.ts
For CJS-only packages, add them to ssr.noExternal for SSR builds

regex: JavaScript heap out of memory|FATAL ERROR.*heap.*allocation
title: Node.js out of memory during build
fixes: Increase Node memory: node --max-old-space-size=4096
Use webpack's cache (filesystem type) to reduce rebuild memory
Split into smaller chunks or reduce source-map detail (eval-source-map)

title: Sass deprecation warnings blocking build
fixes: Migrate from @import to @use/@forward (Sass module system)
Set silenceDeprecations in sass-loader options for temporary suppression
Update sass to dart-sass and fix deprecated division (use math.div())

title: Vite/Rollup plugin not found or misconfigured
fixes: Install the plugin package: npm install -D @vitejs/plugin-react
Check that plugins array in vite.config.ts calls the plugin function: plugin()
Verify plugin is compatible with your Vite version

title: Web Worker build/bundling error
fixes: Use new Worker(new URL('./worker.ts', import.meta.url)) syntax for Vite
For webpack, use worker-loader or asset modules type: 'asset/resource'
Check that worker file doesn't import modules incompatible with worker scope

regex: export.*not found|does not provide an export named
title: Named export not found in module
fixes: Check the package uses named exports — may need default import instead
For CJS packages in Vite, add to optimizeDeps.include for proper ESM wrapping
Verify the exact export name in the package's dist/index file

regex: Asset size limit.*exceeded|entrypoint size limit|WARNING.*size limit
title: Bundle size limit warning
fixes: Enable code splitting with dynamic imports for large dependencies
Analyze bundle with webpack-bundle-analyzer or rollup-plugin-visualizer
Set performance.hints to false in webpack config if limits are acceptable

title: TypeScript errors blocking production build
fixes: Fix type errors — Vite uses tsc for type checking in build mode
Use vite-plugin-checker for non-blocking type errors during dev
As last resort, add // @ts-ignore or set noEmit: true with separate tsc check

title: Build entry point not found
fixes: Check build.rollupOptions.input path matches your entry HTML/JS file
Verify root option in vite.config.ts points to correct directory
Ensure index.html exists in the project root (Vite's default entry)

title: Path alias not resolving in build
fixes: Configure resolve.alias in vite.config.ts AND paths in tsconfig.json
Use vite-tsconfig-paths plugin to auto-sync tsconfig paths
Ensure alias values are absolute paths using path.resolve(__dirname, './src')

title: Jest assertion value mismatch
fixes: Check expected vs actual values — use .toEqual() for objects instead of .toBe()
For floating point, use .toBeCloseTo() instead of exact equality
Verify test setup provides the expected data/mock state

regex: Expected.*mock.*to have been called|not been called|toHaveBeenCalled
title: Jest mock function not called as expected
fixes: Verify the mock is injected correctly — check the module path in jest.mock()
Ensure the code path that calls the function is actually reached
Use mockImplementation to trace when/if the mock gets invoked

title: Jest async test timeout
fixes: Ensure async functions resolve: await the promise or call done()
Increase timeout: jest.setTimeout(10000) or third arg to it()
Check for unresolved promises — missing API mock or dangling await

title: Jest snapshot mismatch
fixes: Review the diff — if change is intentional, update with: jest --updateSnapshot
Avoid snapshots of timestamps, random IDs, or unstable output
Use .toMatchInlineSnapshot() for small, focused snapshot assertions

title: Jest cannot resolve module in test
fixes: Configure moduleNameMapper in jest.config for path aliases
Add moduleDirectories: ['node_modules', 'src'] for absolute imports
Mock non-JS modules (CSS, images) with identity-obj-proxy or empty module

title: Jest coverage threshold not met
fixes: Write tests for uncovered code paths identified in coverage report
Adjust thresholds in jest.config coverageThreshold if temporarily unreachable
Focus on branch coverage — add tests for else/catch/error paths

title: Jest spy on non-existent or non-function property
fixes: Verify the method name exists on the object being spied on
For getters/setters, use jest.spyOn(obj, 'prop', 'get')
Ensure the module is imported before spying — mock at the top level

title: DOM APIs not available in Jest test
fixes: Set testEnvironment: 'jsdom' in jest.config.js
Add @jest-environment jsdom docblock at top of specific test file
Use @testing-library/jest-dom for DOM assertions in jsdom environment

regex: Cannot mock.*ES Module|The module factory.*jest\.mock.*not allowed
title: Jest ES Module mocking limitation
fixes: Use jest.unstable_mockModule() for ESM modules
Move jest.mock() calls to the top of file (before imports) — it's hoisted
Create manual mocks in __mocks__ directory adjacent to the module

title: React/Angular state update not wrapped in act()
fixes: Wrap state-triggering code in act(() => { ... }) or use async act
Use @testing-library's findBy* queries which handle act() internally
Await all promises and timers before assertions: await waitFor(() => ...)

regex: TypeError.*fetch.*not a function|fetch is not defined.*test
title: Fetch API not available in Jest environment
fixes: Mock fetch globally: global.fetch = jest.fn()
Install jest-fetch-mock or msw for comprehensive HTTP mocking
Use @testing-library's server (msw) for realistic API mocking

title: Jest cannot parse ES module syntax
fixes: Configure transform in jest.config to use babel-jest or ts-jest
Add transformIgnorePatterns to transform specific node_modules packages
For ESM packages, add them to transformIgnorePatterns exception list

title: Test state leaking between test cases
fixes: Reset mocks in beforeEach: jest.clearAllMocks() or jest.resetAllMocks()
Don't mutate shared module-level variables — reinitialize in beforeEach
Use jest.isolateModules() to get fresh module instances per test

title: Jest fake timers not advancing
fixes: Call jest.advanceTimersByTime(ms) or jest.runAllTimers() to advance
Use jest.useFakeTimers() before the code that sets timers
For promises + timers combo, await + advanceTimers in a loop or use runAllTimersAsync()

title: Unhandled promise rejection in Jest test
fixes: Add expect(promise).rejects.toThrow() for expected rejections
Wrap async test body in try/catch if testing error paths
Ensure all promises in test are awaited — missing await causes unhandled rejections

title: Jest mock declared after module import
fixes: Move jest.mock() to the top of the file — it's hoisted before imports
For dynamic mocking, use jest.doMock() with require() inside the test
Use jest.resetModules() between tests if changing mock implementations

title: Event listener leak in Jest tests
fixes: Remove event listeners in afterEach or component cleanup
Use --detectOpenHandles flag to identify what's keeping tests alive
Ensure server/socket connections opened in tests are closed in afterAll

title: Jest object equality confusion (toBe vs toEqual)
fixes: Use .toEqual() for deep equality of objects and arrays
.toBe() uses Object.is — same reference required for objects
For partial matching, use expect.objectContaining({key: value})

title: Jest worker process crash
fixes: Increase memory: --max-workers=2 to reduce parallel memory pressure
Run with --runInBand to execute tests serially for debugging
Check for segfaults in native modules — rebuild with: npm rebuild

title: Jest mock not properly initialized
fixes: Ensure jest.mock() path exactly matches the import path in source code
Use jest.fn() to create mock functions before assigning mockReturnValue
Check __mocks__ directory structure mirrors the module path exactly

regex: Error.*Process completed with exit code [1-9]|exit code.*non-zero
title: GitHub Actions step failed with non-zero exit code
fixes: Check the failing step's logs for the actual error message above the exit code
Add 'continue-on-error: true' temporarily to isolate which step fails
Ensure all dependencies are installed and environment variables are set

title: CI pipeline job timeout
fixes: Increase timeout-minutes on the job or specific step
Add caching for dependencies (actions/cache) to speed up installs
Split long-running tests into parallel jobs using matrix strategy

title: GitHub Actions artifact not found
fixes: Ensure upload-artifact and download-artifact use matching artifact names
Check that the upload step ran successfully in a previous job
Verify artifact hasn't expired (default retention: 90 days)

title: CI permission denied error
fixes: Add permissions block to workflow: contents: write, packages: write, etc.
For fork PRs, use pull_request_target event with caution for write access
Check that GITHUB_TOKEN has required scopes for the operation

title: GitHub Actions secret not available
fixes: Secrets aren't available in fork PRs — use pull_request_target or environment protection
Check secret name matches exactly (case-sensitive) in Settings > Secrets
Ensure the secret is scoped to the correct environment if using environments

title: CI cache miss — dependencies not cached
fixes: Verify cache key includes lock file hash: hashFiles('**/package-lock.json')
Check that the cache path matches where your package manager stores dependencies
Ensure restore-keys provides fallback patterns for partial cache hits

title: Docker image pull failure in CI
fixes: Authenticate to container registry before pull: docker login
Check image tag exists — may have been deleted or never pushed
For GHCR, use actions/login-action with GITHUB_TOKEN

title: CI runner out of disk space
fixes: Free space with: sudo rm -rf /usr/local/lib/android /usr/share/dotnet
Use actions/setup-* with smaller tool versions
Split build artifacts and upload early — don't accumulate on disk

title: GitHub API rate limit hit in CI
fixes: Use GITHUB_TOKEN instead of PAT for higher rate limits (1000/hr vs 5000/hr)
Add retry logic with exponential backoff for API calls
Cache API responses (e.g., release assets) to reduce call frequency

title: GitHub Actions workflow not triggered
fixes: Check event trigger type and branch filter match (on: push, branches: [main])
Workflow files must be on the default branch to be recognized
Verify YAML syntax — invalid YAML silently prevents workflow registration

title: GitHub Actions matrix strategy failure
fixes: Check matrix values are valid YAML arrays and combinations are correct
Use fail-fast: false to continue other matrix jobs when one fails
Verify matrix.include/exclude syntax matches GitHub's expected format

title: CI workflow cancelled by concurrency group
fixes: This is expected with concurrency + cancel-in-progress: true
Remove cancel-in-progress if you need all runs to complete
Use unique concurrency group keys per branch: ${{ github.ref }}

title: Self-hosted runner not available
fixes: Check runner is online in Settings > Actions > Runners
Verify runner labels match the runs-on value in workflow
Restart the runner service or re-register if it went offline

title: Deployment step failed in CI
fixes: Check deployment credentials/tokens are valid and not expired
Verify the target environment is reachable from the CI runner
Add health check after deploy and automatic rollback on failure

title: GitHub Actions YAML syntax error
fixes: Validate YAML with actionlint or yamllint before pushing
Check indentation — YAML uses spaces only, no tabs
Ensure multiline strings use | or > block scalars properly

regex: action.*version.*not found|Unable to resolve action|uses.*not found
title: GitHub Action reference not found
fixes: Pin to a specific version/SHA: uses: actions/checkout@v4
Check the action repository exists and the tag/branch is published
For private actions, ensure the workflow has access to the repo

title: npm ci fails due to lockfile mismatch
fixes: Regenerate lockfile: delete package-lock.json, run npm install, commit
Ensure node version in CI matches local (use .nvmrc or engines field)
Use --legacy-peer-deps if peer dependency conflicts block install

title: CI service container unhealthy
fixes: Add health-check options with retries and interval to the service definition
Ensure the service image tag exists and is compatible with runner architecture
Wait for service readiness before running steps: use wait-for-it or pg_isready

title: GitHub Actions OIDC token request failure
fixes: Add permissions: id-token: write to the job or workflow
Configure the cloud provider's federated identity to trust the GitHub OIDC issuer
Check the subject claim filter matches your repo/branch/environment

title: Composite action definition error
fixes: Ensure action.yml has required fields: name, description, runs.using: composite
All inputs referenced in steps must be declared in inputs section
Use ${{ inputs.name }} syntax to reference inputs in composite steps

title: Electron IPC communication failure
fixes: Expose IPC methods via preload script using contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld()
Register ipcMain.handle() in main process before renderer sends invoke()
Don't access ipcRenderer directly — use preload bridge with contextIsolation: true

title: Electron operation on destroyed window
fixes: Check win.isDestroyed() before calling methods on the window
Remove event listeners that reference the window in the 'closed' event
Use weak references or null the variable when window is destroyed

title: Electron native module build/compatibility error
fixes: Rebuild native modules for Electron's Node version: electron-rebuild
Use @electron/rebuild or postinstall script: electron-rebuild -f
Check that native module supports the target Electron version and architecture

title: Electron auto-update failure
fixes: Ensure app-update.yml is included in build output (electron-builder config)
Check that update server URL is accessible and returns valid release info
Code-sign the app — unsigned apps can't use auto-update on macOS

title: Electron ASAR archive file access error
fixes: Use path.join(process.resourcesPath, ...) for files outside the ASAR
Add files to asarUnpack in electron-builder config for native access
Don't use fs.existsSync on asar paths — use electron's asar-aware fs

title: Electron packaging/distribution build failure
fixes: Check electron-builder config in package.json for valid target configuration
For NSIS errors on Windows, ensure NSIS is accessible or use portable target
For macOS DMG, ensure you have valid signing certificate or disable signing for dev

title: Electron security configuration error
fixes: Set contextIsolation: true and nodeIntegration: false (secure defaults)
Use preload scripts with contextBridge to safely expose APIs to renderer
Enable sandbox: true for additional process isolation

title: Electron screen/display API error
fixes: Access screen module only after app 'ready' event fires
Import screen from electron in main process only: const { screen } = require('electron')
Handle display-removed event for multi-monitor setups

title: Electron session/webRequest error
fixes: Access session from mainWindow.webContents.session, not before window creation
Register webRequest interceptors after session is created and before navigation
Use partition for isolated sessions: new BrowserWindow({ webPreferences: { partition: 'persist:app' } })

title: Electron Menu template error
fixes: Validate menu template structure: { label, submenu, click, accelerator }
Use role shortcuts for standard items: 'copy', 'paste', 'quit'
Build menu from template after app is ready: Menu.setApplicationMenu(menu)

title: Electron custom protocol registration error
fixes: Register protocols before app 'ready' event using protocol.registerSchemesAsPrivileged()
Use protocol.handle() (Electron 25+) instead of deprecated registerFileProtocol
Set scheme privileges: { standard: true, secure: true } for full URL API support

title: Electron Tray/Notification system error
fixes: Keep tray variable reference at module level to prevent garbage collection
Use nativeImage.createFromPath() with absolute path to icon file
Check Notification.isSupported() before creating notifications

title: Electron renderer process crash
fixes: Listen for 'render-process-gone' event and offer to reload the window
Check renderer memory usage — large datasets may cause OOM crashes
Enable crashReporter to collect and analyze crash dumps

title: Electron dialog used in wrong process
fixes: Use dialog module in main process only, invoke via IPC from renderer
Call dialog.showOpenDialog(mainWindow, options) from an ipcMain handler
In renderer, send IPC message and receive file paths from main process response

title: Electron power monitor error
fixes: Access powerMonitor only after app 'ready' event
Use powerMonitor.getSystemIdleTime() for idle detection (returns seconds)
Listen to 'suspend'/'resume' events to handle sleep/wake gracefully

title: Electron SSL certificate verification error
fixes: Handle 'certificate-error' event on the app to allow specific certs in dev
Install proper CA certificates in the system trust store
Do NOT set rejectUnauthorized: false in production — fix the cert chain instead

title: Electron deep link / custom URL scheme not working
fixes: Register protocol handler in electron-builder config: protocols.schemes
Call app.setAsDefaultProtocolClient('myapp') at startup
Handle 'open-url' event (macOS) or process second-instance args (Windows/Linux)

title: Electron webContents navigation failure
fixes: Listen for 'did-fail-load' event to handle navigation errors gracefully
Check will-navigate event isn't blocking the navigation
Verify the URL is accessible and not blocked by webRequest interceptors

title: Electron safeStorage/keychain error
fixes: Check safeStorage.isEncryptionAvailable() before using encrypt/decrypt
On Linux, ensure a keyring service (gnome-keyring, kwallet) is available
Fall back to less secure storage if safeStorage is unavailable in the environment

title: Electron clipboard/image operation error
fixes: Check nativeImage is not empty before writing to clipboard
Use supported formats: PNG for images, UTF-8 text, HTML for rich text
Ensure clipboard operations run in main process or through IPC bridge

title: Metro bundler crash or port conflict
fixes: Kill existing Metro process: npx kill-port 8081
Clear Metro cache: npx react-native start --reset-cache
Check for syntax errors in recently modified files — Metro crashes on parse errors

title: React Native native module not found
fixes: Run npx pod-install (iOS) or rebuild the project after adding native deps
For New Architecture, ensure the module implements TurboModule interface
Check autolinking: npx react-native config to verify the module is detected

title: React Native Android Gradle build failure
fixes: Run cd android && ./gradlew clean then rebuild
Check android/gradle.properties for JVM memory: org.gradle.jvmargs=-Xmx4g
Verify compileSdkVersion and targetSdkVersion match installed SDK versions

title: iOS code signing / provisioning error
fixes: Open Xcode, select team in Signing & Capabilities, let Xcode manage signing
Ensure the provisioning profile matches the bundle identifier
For CI, install certificates/profiles in keychain before build

title: Flipper debugging tool error
fixes: Update Flipper pods: FLIPPER_VERSION in Podfile to match installed Flipper
Remove Flipper from production builds — it's dev-only
Disable Flipper if not needed: remove use_flipper! from Podfile and pod install

title: Hermes engine error
fixes: Enable Hermes in android/app/build.gradle: hermesEnabled = true
For iOS, ensure hermes_enabled = true in Podfile and run pod install
Clear Hermes bytecode cache: npx react-native start --reset-cache

title: React Native view manager not registered
fixes: Rebuild the native project after installing a UI library
Check that the native module's ViewManager is registered in the package
For iOS, run pod install. For Android, sync gradle and rebuild

title: React Native FlatList rendering issue
fixes: Add keyExtractor prop and ensure it returns unique stable strings
Set getItemLayout for fixed-height items to skip measurement
Use windowSize, maxToRenderPerBatch, and removeClippedSubviews for large lists

title: React Native network request failure
fixes: For iOS, add App Transport Security exception in Info.plist for HTTP
Check Android network_security_config.xml allows cleartext if using HTTP
Verify the emulator/device has network access — use device IP, not localhost

title: React Navigation routing error
fixes: Ensure NavigationContainer wraps the entire app at root level
Check route names match exactly between navigator definition and navigate() calls
Don't navigate from unmounted components — check isFocused or use useFocusEffect

title: iOS CocoaPods installation error
fixes: Run: cd ios && pod install --repo-update
If CDN fails, switch to trunk: pod repo remove trunk then pod install
Delete ios/Pods and ios/Podfile.lock then run pod install fresh

title: iOS duplicate symbol / linking error
fixes: Check for duplicate library imports in Podfile — remove manual linking if using autolinking
Clean derived data: rm -rf ~/Library/Developer/Xcode/DerivedData
In Build Phases, check for duplicate frameworks in Link Binary With Libraries

regex: Unable to load script.*bundle|Could not connect to development server
title: React Native bundle loading failure
fixes: Ensure Metro bundler is running: npx react-native start
For physical device, set the correct IP in the dev menu (shake device)
For Android emulator, run: adb reverse tcp:8081 tcp:8081

title: React Native Animated native driver error
fixes: Not all styles support useNativeDriver: true (only transform and opacity do)
Set useNativeDriver: false for layout animations (width, height, margin)
Use react-native-reanimated for complex animations with native thread support

title: React Native AsyncStorage error
fixes: Install correctly: npm install @react-native-async-storage/async-storage
Run pod install for iOS after installing the package
Don't store large data in AsyncStorage — use SQLite or MMKV for large datasets

title: React Native Android permission denied
fixes: Add permission to AndroidManifest.xml
For runtime permissions (camera, location), use PermissionsAndroid.request()
On Android 11+, check for new scoped storage restrictions for file access

title: React Native New Architecture (Fabric/TurboModules) error
fixes: Enable New Architecture: newArchEnabled=true in gradle.properties (Android)
For iOS, set RCT_NEW_ARCH_ENABLED=1 in pod install
Check that all dependencies support New Architecture — some need native code updates

title: React Native Android device/emulator connection error
fixes: Check device is connected: adb devices (should list your device)
Enable USB debugging on physical device in Developer Options
For emulator, ensure it's running: emulator -list-avds then emulator @name

title: React Native image asset resolution error
fixes: Use require('./path/to/image.png') with correct relative path from component file
For Android, ensure images are in android/app/src/main/res/drawable* folders
Clear Metro cache after adding new images: npx react-native start --reset-cache

title: React Native file watcher error (Watchman)
fixes: Increase inotify watchers: echo fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288 | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf
Restart Watchman: watchman shutdown-server then start Metro again
On macOS increase file limits: ulimit -n 4096 or install latest Watchman via brew

regex: Type '.*' is not assignable to type 'never'
title: Conditional type resolves to never
fixes: Check that your conditional type branches cover all cases
Add explicit type annotations to help TypeScript resolve the conditional
Use Extract/Exclude utility types to narrow the union before conditional evaluation

regex: Type instantiation is excessively deep and possibly infinite
title: Recursive conditional type too deep
fixes: Add a recursion depth limit counter to your recursive conditional type
Use tail-call optimization pattern with accumulator type parameter
Simplify the type by breaking it into smaller non-recursive helpers

regex: infer.*can only be used in.*extends clause
title: Infer keyword used outside extends clause
fixes: Move the infer keyword inside a conditional type's extends clause: T extends infer U ? U : never
Wrap your type in a conditional: type Result = T extends Array<infer U> ? U : T
Use a helper conditional type that performs the inference

regex: Cannot find module '.*' or its corresponding type declarations
title: Path mapping not resolving module
fixes: Verify paths in tsconfig.json match the baseUrl-relative import pattern
Ensure baseUrl is set correctly in tsconfig.json
Add the path alias to both tsconfig.json and tsconfig.app.json (or tsconfig.build.json)

title: Declaration merging conflict
fixes: Ensure merged declarations are in the same module scope (both global or both module-scoped)
Check that interface properties don't conflict with incompatible types across declarations
Use module augmentation syntax: declare module 'package' { interface Existing { ... } }

regex: Property '.*' does not exist on type '.*\[keyof.*\]'
title: Mapped type indexing loses type narrowing
fixes: Use a generic function with K extends keyof T to preserve the specific key type
Add an explicit type assertion or use a conditional type to narrow the mapped result
Index with a specific literal type instead of the broad keyof union

regex: Template literal type.*produces a union type that is too complex
title: Template literal type combinatorial explosion
fixes: Reduce the number of union members in the template literal constituents
Split the type into smaller template literals and intersect or union them manually
Use a branded string type instead of an exhaustive template literal for large combinations

title: Project references missing composite flag
fixes: Add "composite": true to the referenced project's tsconfig.json compilerOptions
Also add "declaration": true which is required when composite is enabled
Run tsc --build to build project references in dependency order

regex: Unable to resolve signature of.*decorator when called as an expression
title: Decorator metadata reflection error
fixes: Enable "emitDecoratorMetadata": true and "experimentalDecorators": true in tsconfig.json
Ensure reflect-metadata is imported at the application entry point
Check that the decorator factory returns the correct signature (ClassDecorator, PropertyDecorator, etc.)

regex: Module '.*' has no exported member '.*'.*Did you mean to use 'import.*from'
title: Module augmentation not found
fixes: Ensure your augmentation file uses 'declare module' with the exact module specifier string
The augmentation file must contain at least one top-level import or export to be treated as a module
Check that the .d.ts file with the augmentation is included in tsconfig's include or files array

regex: Type '.*' does not satisfy the constraint '.*'.*infer
title: Infer constraint not satisfied in conditional type
fixes: Add an explicit constraint to the infer clause: infer U extends string
Wrap the inferred type in a conditional check before using it
Ensure the input type actually extends the pattern you're matching against

regex: A mapped type may not declare properties or methods
title: Invalid mapped type syntax with extra properties
fixes: Use intersection type to combine mapped type with additional properties: MappedType & { extra: string }
Move additional properties to a separate interface and intersect
Use the newer key remapping syntax: [K in keyof T as NewKey]: T[K]

regex: Cannot use namespace '.*' as a type
title: Namespace used as type instead of typeof
fixes: Use typeof Namespace to get the type of the namespace object
Import the specific type from the namespace: Namespace.TypeName
If augmenting, use 'declare namespace' not 'declare module' for non-module scripts

regex: Output file '.*' has not been built from source file '.*'.*project reference
title: Project reference output stale or missing
fixes: Run tsc --build --force to rebuild all project references from scratch
Delete the tsconfig.tsbuildinfo file and rebuild
Ensure declarationMap is enabled if you need source navigation across references

title: Deferred conditional type cannot be used directly
fixes: Add a type constraint to help TypeScript eagerly resolve the conditional
Use a generic function instead of a type alias to defer evaluation to call site
Extract the conditional into a helper type and apply it at a point where T is known

regex: An index signature parameter type cannot be a literal type or generic type
title: Mapped type needed instead of index signature
fixes: Use a mapped type [K in 'key1' | 'key2']: ValueType instead of index signature
Use Record<'key1' | 'key2', ValueType> utility type
For generic keys, use a mapped type: { [K in T]: ValueType }

title: Decorator applied to invalid target
fixes: Ensure the decorator is applied to a class, method, accessor, property, or parameter (not a standalone function)
Check that the decorator signature matches the target: ClassDecorator, MethodDecorator, PropertyDecorator, or ParameterDecorator
Arrow functions and function declarations cannot have decorators — use class methods instead

regex: The 'this' context of type '.*' is not assignable to method's 'this' of type
title: This-type mismatch in mapped/conditional type
fixes: Use an arrow function to preserve the lexical 'this' context
Add an explicit this parameter to the function signature: method(this: CorrectType, ...)
Bind the method in the constructor or use a class property with arrow function

title: Reflect API not available for decorator metadata
fixes: Install and import reflect-metadata: import 'reflect-metadata' at the top of your entry file
Add "types": ["reflect-metadata"] to tsconfig.json compilerOptions
Install the package: npm install reflect-metadata

title: Recursive mapped type exceeds depth limit
fixes: Add a depth counter generic parameter and terminate recursion at a fixed depth
Use interface extends instead of type aliases for recursive structures (interfaces are lazily evaluated)
Simplify the type by reducing nesting or using any at deep recursion boundaries

title: EPIPE error - writing to closed pipe/socket
fixes: Handle the 'error' event on the writable stream before writing
Check stream.writable before calling write()
Use stream.destroy() gracefully and handle the 'close' event to stop writes

title: ECONNRESET - connection forcibly closed by remote
fixes: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient network failures
Handle the 'error' event on the socket/request and log details for debugging
Use keep-alive with appropriate timeout settings to detect dead connections early

regex: FATAL ERROR:.*JavaScript heap out of memory
title: Node.js memory leak - heap out of memory
fixes: Use --max-old-space-size=4096 to increase heap limit temporarily while investigating
Profile with node --inspect and Chrome DevTools Memory tab to find retained objects
Check for growing arrays, Maps, event listener leaks, or closures capturing large scopes

title: Event loop blocked by synchronous operation
fixes: Move CPU-intensive work to a Worker thread using worker_threads module
Break large synchronous loops into batches using setImmediate() between chunks
Use streaming APIs instead of loading entire files into memory synchronously

title: Stream push after end-of-file signal
fixes: Ensure push(null) is only called once and no more data is pushed after it
Check your _read() implementation for race conditions that push after stream end
Use a flag to track whether the stream has been ended before pushing data

title: Cluster worker crashed unexpectedly
fixes: Listen for 'exit' event on cluster workers and fork a replacement: cluster.on('exit', (worker) => cluster.fork())
Add uncaughtException and unhandledRejection handlers in worker processes
Implement graceful shutdown: worker.disconnect() before process.exit() to finish pending requests

title: Unhandled promise rejection crashing process
fixes: Add .catch() to every promise chain, or use try/catch with async/await
Set global handler: process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason, promise) => { log and gracefully shut down })
In Node 15+, unhandled rejections crash by default — ensure all async paths have error handling

title: CORS middleware order or configuration issue
fixes: Ensure cors() middleware is registered BEFORE your route handlers in Express
For credentials, set { origin: 'http://specific-domain.com', credentials: true } — wildcard '*' won't work with credentials
Handle OPTIONS preflight explicitly or ensure cors middleware runs for all methods including OPTIONS

title: EventEmitter memory leak - too many listeners
fixes: Remove listeners in cleanup: emitter.removeListener() or emitter.off() when done
If legitimate, increase limit: emitter.setMaxListeners(n) with a comment explaining why
Check for repeated addEventListener calls in loops without corresponding removal

title: Socket hang up - premature connection close
fixes: Increase server timeout: server.timeout = 120000 for long-running requests
Handle the 'error' event on both request and response objects
Check if a reverse proxy (nginx) is closing the connection before Node finishes — increase proxy_read_timeout

regex: ERR_HTTP_HEADERS_SENT.*Cannot set headers after they are sent
title: Headers already sent - double response
fixes: Add return statement after res.send()/res.json()/res.redirect() to prevent further execution
Check async callbacks for multiple paths that call res.send()
Use a flag or early return pattern in middleware to ensure only one response is sent

title: Connection timeout to external service
fixes: Set explicit timeout on HTTP requests: { timeout: 5000 } and handle the timeout event
Implement circuit breaker pattern to fail fast when service is consistently unavailable
Check DNS resolution and network connectivity; use keep-alive agent to reuse connections

title: System out of memory for child process or buffer
fixes: Reduce buffer sizes and use streaming instead of buffering entire payloads
