Increase system swap or available RAM; check for memory leaks in long-running processes
Use { maxBuffer: 1024 * 1024 * 50 } for child_process.exec if output is legitimately large

title: File descriptor limit exhausted
fixes: Use graceful-fs or limit concurrent file operations with a semaphore/queue (e.g., p-limit)
Increase OS file descriptor limit: ulimit -n 65536
Ensure all file handles are closed properly — use try/finally or streams with autoClose: true

title: Stream closed before pipeline finished
fixes: Use stream.pipeline() instead of manual pipe() — it handles cleanup and error propagation
Handle 'error' and 'close' events on all streams in the pipeline
Check that the readable source isn't being destroyed before the writable finishes draining

title: Express body parser payload limit exceeded
fixes: Increase the limit: app.use(express.json({ limit: '10mb' }))
For file uploads, use multer with appropriate fileSize limits instead of body-parser
Implement chunked upload for large files instead of sending entire payload at once

regex: Error: self signed certificate in certificate chain
title: Node.js rejecting self-signed certificate
fixes: For development only: set NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 (NEVER in production)
Add the CA cert to the request agent: new https.Agent({ ca: fs.readFileSync('ca.pem') })
Install the self-signed CA into the system trust store or Node's --use-openssl-ca

title: Port already in use - address bind failed
fixes: Kill the existing process: lsof -i :PORT then kill -9 PID (or netstat -tlnp on Linux)
Use a different port or implement automatic port finding with portfinder package
Enable SO_REUSEADDR: server.listen(port, { exclusive: false }) for cluster mode

title: Stack overflow from deep recursion
fixes: Convert recursion to iteration using an explicit stack (array)
Use setImmediate() to break deep recursion into async ticks
Increase stack size with --stack-size=65536 flag (not a real fix — refactor the algorithm)

title: Client aborted request before response complete
fixes: Listen for 'close' event on req to detect client disconnection and stop processing
Use AbortController/AbortSignal to cancel downstream operations when client disconnects
Return early in middleware if req.destroyed is true before doing expensive work

title: PostgreSQL deadlock detected
fixes: Ensure all transactions access tables and rows in a consistent order
Reduce transaction duration by doing non-DB work outside the transaction
Implement retry logic — deadlocks are transient and the aborted transaction can be retried

regex: ERROR.*could not serialize access due to concurrent update
title: PostgreSQL serialization failure
fixes: Retry the transaction — serialization failures are expected in SERIALIZABLE isolation
Use SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to explicitly lock rows you intend to modify
Consider downgrading to REPEATABLE READ if strict serializability isn't required

title: PostgreSQL column does not exist
fixes: Check for case sensitivity — unquoted identifiers are lowercased: use double quotes for mixed-case columns
Verify the column exists in the correct table with \d tablename in psql
Run pending migrations that add the column, or check if an alias is needed in the query

title: PostgreSQL table/relation not found
fixes: Check the search_path: SET search_path TO my_schema, public
Verify the table exists and you're connected to the correct database
Run pending migrations to create the table, or qualify with schema: schema_name.table_name

title: PostgreSQL permission denied
fixes: Grant the required permission: GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON table TO role
Check if the role has USAGE permission on the schema: GRANT USAGE ON SCHEMA public TO role
Verify you're connected as the correct user and the table owner matches expectations

title: pg_dump connection failure
fixes: Verify PostgreSQL is running and accepting connections on the specified host/port
Check pg_hba.conf allows connections from the pg_dump client IP
Use correct connection params: pg_dump -h host -p 5432 -U user -d dbname

title: PostgreSQL replication lag
fixes: Check replica performance: SELECT now() - pg_last_xact_replay_timestamp() AS lag
Increase wal_keep_size or use replication slots to prevent WAL segment removal
Check for long-running queries on replica blocking WAL replay — cancel them or set hot_standby_feedback=on

title: PostgreSQL vacuum blocked or failing
fixes: Reduce long-running transactions that hold locks preventing vacuum
Increase autovacuum_vacuum_cost_delay to reduce I/O impact or decrease it to vacuum faster
For emergency bloat, use pg_repack extension which doesn't hold exclusive locks

regex: ERROR.*value too long for type character varying\((\d+)\)
title: PostgreSQL varchar length exceeded
fixes: Truncate the input value to fit the column's character limit
Alter the column to increase the length: ALTER TABLE t ALTER COLUMN c TYPE varchar(500)
Use TEXT type instead of varchar(n) if there's no business reason for the length limit

regex: ERROR.*duplicate key value violates unique constraint
title: PostgreSQL unique constraint violation
fixes: Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (column) DO UPDATE SET ... for upsert behavior
Check for race conditions in concurrent inserts — use advisory locks or serializable isolation
Verify the conflicting value and decide whether to update, skip (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), or error

title: PostgreSQL out of shared memory for locks
fixes: Increase max_locks_per_transaction in postgresql.conf
Reduce the number of tables affected in a single transaction (partition operations are common culprits)
Restart PostgreSQL after changing the shared memory configuration

title: PostgreSQL connection limit reached
fixes: Increase connection limit: ALTER ROLE myuser CONNECTION LIMIT 100
Use a connection pooler like PgBouncer to multiplex connections
Close idle connections and check for connection leaks in application code

regex: ERROR.*cannot drop.*because other objects depend on it
title: PostgreSQL drop blocked by dependencies
fixes: Use CASCADE to drop dependent objects: DROP TABLE t CASCADE (review what will be dropped first)
Query pg_depend to see what depends on the object before dropping
Drop dependent views/foreign keys manually first if CASCADE is too aggressive

regex: ERROR.*prepared transaction.*already exists|ERROR.*current transaction is aborted
title: PostgreSQL aborted transaction state
fixes: Issue ROLLBACK to clear the aborted transaction state before new queries
Use SAVEPOINT within transactions to allow partial rollback without aborting the whole transaction
Check for errors in previous statements in the transaction block that caused the abort

regex: ERROR.*could not resize shared memory segment.*No space left
title: PostgreSQL shared memory allocation failure
fixes: Increase kernel shared memory limits: sysctl -w kernel.shmmax=17179869184
Check /dev/shm usage and increase its size in /etc/fstab
Reduce shared_buffers or other shared memory parameters if system resources are limited

title: PostgreSQL type cast failure
fixes: Validate and sanitize input data before inserting — ensure UUIDs/integers/timestamps are properly formatted
Use explicit type casting with error handling: CAST(value AS type) or value::type
Check for empty strings being passed where NULL is expected — use NULLIF('', value)

regex: pg_dump.*aborting because of server version mismatch
title: pg_dump version mismatch with server
fixes: Install pg_dump matching the server version — use the PostgreSQL apt/yum repo for that version
Use the pg_dump binary from the server's installation directory
Upgrade the client tools: apt install postgresql-client-15 (match your server version)

regex: ERROR.*could not open file.*for reading.*Permission denied
title: PostgreSQL COPY command file permission error
fixes: Use COPY ... FROM STDIN with psql's \copy command which runs as the client user, not postgres
Ensure the file is readable by the postgres system user when using server-side COPY
Move the file to /tmp or a directory accessible by the postgres process

title: PostgreSQL index creation error
fixes: Use CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS to avoid errors on existing indexes
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY cannot run inside a transaction — remove BEGIN/COMMIT wrapper
If a concurrent index build failed, drop the INVALID index first: DROP INDEX CONCURRENTLY idx_name

regex: ERROR.*WAL.*segment.*not found|requested WAL segment.*has already been removed
title: PostgreSQL WAL segment removed before replica consumed it
fixes: Use replication slots to retain WAL segments until replicas have consumed them
Increase wal_keep_size (or wal_keep_segments in older versions) to retain more WAL
Rebuild the replica from a fresh base backup if it's fallen too far behind

title: MySQL max connections exceeded
fixes: Increase max_connections: SET GLOBAL max_connections = 500
Use connection pooling in the application to limit concurrent connections
Check for connection leaks: SHOW PROCESSLIST and kill idle connections with KILL <id>

regex: Lock wait timeout exceeded.*try restarting transaction
title: MySQL InnoDB lock wait timeout
fixes: Retry the transaction — another transaction is holding a conflicting lock
Check blocking transactions: SELECT * FROM information_schema.INNODB_TRX
Increase innodb_lock_wait_timeout if workload legitimately needs longer waits, or optimize the blocking query

regex: Cannot add or update a child row.*foreign key constraint fails
title: MySQL foreign key constraint violation on insert/update
fixes: Ensure the referenced parent row exists before inserting the child row
Check that the foreign key column values match the parent table's primary/unique key values exactly (type and value)
Temporarily disable FK checks for bulk loads: SET FOREIGN_KEY_CHECKS=0 (re-enable after)

regex: Cannot delete or update a parent row.*foreign key constraint fails
title: MySQL foreign key blocks parent row deletion
fixes: Delete or update child rows first before deleting the parent
Add ON DELETE CASCADE to the foreign key to auto-delete children
Use ON DELETE SET NULL if child rows should remain but with a null reference

title: MySQL packet too large
fixes: Increase max_allowed_packet: SET GLOBAL max_allowed_packet = 64*1024*1024
Break large INSERTs into smaller batches instead of one massive statement
For large BLOBs, consider streaming to object storage and storing only the reference in MySQL

title: MySQL binary logging unsafe statement
fixes: Switch to ROW-based replication: SET GLOBAL binlog_format = 'ROW'
Avoid non-deterministic functions (UUID(), NOW(), RAND()) in statements with STATEMENT format
Use MIXED format as compromise: SET GLOBAL binlog_format = 'MIXED'

title: MySQL replication SQL thread error
fixes: Check the exact error: SHOW SLAVE STATUS\G — look at Last_SQL_Error
Skip the problematic event if safe: SET GLOBAL sql_slave_skip_counter = 1; START SLAVE
For serious drift, rebuild replica from a fresh backup with CHANGE MASTER TO

title: MySQL table not found
fixes: Check the database context: USE correct_database before the query
Verify table name case sensitivity — Linux MySQL is case-sensitive for table names by default
Run pending migrations or check if the table was dropped/renamed

regex: Deadlock found when trying to get lock.*try restarting transaction
title: MySQL InnoDB deadlock
fixes: Implement retry logic — InnoDB automatically rolls back the victim transaction
Access tables and rows in a consistent order across all transactions
Keep transactions short and avoid user interaction within transaction boundaries

title: MySQL table full - disk or tmp table limit
fixes: Check disk space: df -h — free up space or add storage
For tmp tables: increase tmp_table_size and max_heap_table_size
For InnoDB: check innodb_data_file_path autoextend is enabled, or add data files

title: MySQL access denied
fixes: Verify username, password, and host: mysql -u user -p -h host
Grant permissions: GRANT ALL ON database.* TO 'user'@'host' IDENTIFIED BY 'password'; FLUSH PRIVILEGES
Check that the user exists for the connecting host: SELECT user, host FROM mysql.user

title: MySQL character encoding mismatch
fixes: Convert column to utf8mb4: ALTER TABLE t MODIFY col VARCHAR(255) CHARACTER SET utf8mb4
Ensure connection charset matches: SET NAMES utf8mb4
Check that the database and table defaults are utf8mb4: ALTER DATABASE db CHARACTER SET utf8mb4 COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci

regex: Row size too large.*maximum row size.*InnoDB
title: MySQL InnoDB row size limit exceeded
fixes: Use DYNAMIC or COMPRESSED row format: ALTER TABLE t ROW_FORMAT=DYNAMIC
Change large VARCHAR columns to TEXT/BLOB which are stored off-page
Set innodb_page_size=32K or reduce the number of columns per table

title: MySQL replication IO thread error
fixes: Check network connectivity between master and replica
Verify replication user credentials and REPLICATION SLAVE grant on master
If binary logs were purged, rebuild replica: STOP SLAVE; RESET SLAVE ALL; restore from backup

title: MySQL lock wait timeout during DDL or DML
fixes: Find the blocking transaction: SELECT * FROM information_schema.INNODB_LOCK_WAITS
Kill the blocking connection if it's idle: KILL <blocking_trx_id>
Use pt-online-schema-change for DDL on large tables to avoid long locks

title: MySQL data truncation on insert/update
fixes: Check the column type and adjust input data to fit (e.g., truncate string, fix date format)
Alter column to accommodate larger values: ALTER TABLE t MODIFY col VARCHAR(500)
Disable strict mode temporarily for import: SET sql_mode = '' (re-enable after, not recommended for production)

regex: ERROR.*Can't create table.*errno: 150|Foreign key constraint is incorrectly formed
title: MySQL foreign key creation failure
fixes: Ensure parent and child columns have identical data types (including sign, length, and charset)
Both tables must use InnoDB engine for foreign keys
The referenced column must have an index (PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE) on the parent table

regex: ERROR.*server has gone away|MySQL server has gone away
title: MySQL connection dropped - server gone away
fixes: Increase wait_timeout and interactive_timeout for long-idle connections
Implement connection pool health checks and reconnection logic in the application
Check max_allowed_packet if the error occurs during large queries

title: MySQL duplicate key on insert
fixes: Use INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE to handle upserts
Use INSERT IGNORE to silently skip duplicates (data is discarded)
Check the unique index columns and deduplicate input data before inserting

regex: ERROR.*The used command is not allowed with this MySQL version|binlog.*disabled
title: MySQL binary logging disabled or command restricted
fixes: Enable binary logging: add log-bin=mysql-bin to my.cnf and restart MySQL
Set server-id to a unique value when enabling binlog: server-id=1
Check that the user has SUPER or REPLICATION CLIENT privilege for binlog operations

title: Nginx 502 Bad Gateway - upstream unavailable
fixes: Verify the upstream server is running and listening on the expected address/port
Increase proxy_read_timeout and proxy_connect_timeout for slow backends
Check upstream server logs for crashes or OOM kills; increase keepalive connections in upstream block

title: Nginx 504 Gateway Timeout
fixes: Increase proxy_read_timeout (default 60s): proxy_read_timeout 300s
Optimize the backend endpoint that's taking too long to respond
Add proxy_buffering off for streaming endpoints, or increase proxy_buffer_size for large headers

regex: rewrite or internal redirection cycle|return 301.*too many redirects
title: Nginx redirect loop
fixes: Check for conflicting rewrite rules that redirect back and forth (HTTP→HTTPS→HTTP)
Use 'return 301' instead of 'rewrite' for simple redirects to avoid rule interaction
Add a condition to prevent re-redirecting: if ($scheme = https) { ... } or use separate server blocks

title: Nginx SSL certificate loading error
fixes: Verify the certificate file path and that nginx user has read permission
Ensure the certificate file contains the full chain (server cert + intermediates) in PEM format
Check that the private key matches the certificate: openssl x509 -noout -modulus -in cert.pem | md5sum vs openssl rsa -noout -modulus -in key.pem | md5sum

regex: location.*directive is not allowed here|duplicate location
title: Nginx location block configuration error
fixes: Ensure location blocks are inside a server block, not at the http level
Remove duplicate location blocks — merge their contents into one
Check location priority: exact (=) > prefix (^~) > regex (~) > prefix (longest match)

regex: proxy_pass.*no resolver defined|could not be resolved
title: Nginx proxy_pass DNS resolution failure
fixes: Add resolver directive: resolver 8.8.8.8 valid=30s for dynamic upstream DNS
Use a variable in proxy_pass to force runtime resolution: set $backend http://host; proxy_pass $backend
For static upstreams, use IP addresses or ensure /etc/hosts has the entry

regex: client intended to send too large body|413 Request Entity Too Large
title: Nginx 413 payload too large
fixes: Increase client_max_body_size: client_max_body_size 100m in http/server/location block
For file uploads, also set it in the specific location block that handles uploads
Set client_max_body_size 0 to disable the check entirely (use application-level validation instead)

title: Nginx upstream connection refused
fixes: Verify the backend service is running: systemctl status app or check the process
Check the upstream port matches what the backend is actually listening on
If using Unix sockets, verify the socket file exists and has correct permissions

title: Nginx permission denied serving static files
fixes: Ensure nginx worker process user (typically www-data/nginx) can read the files: chmod -R o+r /path
Check all parent directories have execute permission: chmod o+x on each directory in the path
Verify SELinux context if on RHEL/CentOS: restorecon -Rv /path or set httpd_sys_content_t

regex: no live upstreams while connecting to upstream
title: Nginx all upstream servers marked down
fixes: Check health of upstream servers — all have failed health checks or max_fails threshold
Increase max_fails or fail_timeout to be more tolerant: server backend1 max_fails=5 fail_timeout=30s
Add more upstream servers for redundancy or set backup servers

regex: AH01630.*client denied by server configuration|403 Forbidden
title: Apache 403 Forbidden - directory access denied
fixes: Update Directory directive: <Directory /path> Require all granted </Directory>
In Apache 2.4+, use Require instead of Order/Allow/Deny (2.2 syntax)
Check .htaccess for Deny rules and ensure AllowOverride is enabled if using .htaccess

title: Apache request body size limit exceeded
fixes: Increase LimitRequestBody in the Directory or Location block: LimitRequestBody 104857600
For PHP uploads, also set php.ini: upload_max_filesize and post_max_size
Set LimitRequestBody 0 to remove the limit (validate at application level)

title: Apache 503 Service Unavailable - backend down
fixes: Verify the backend application is running on the ProxyPass target
Increase ProxyTimeout and timeout settings for slow backends
Check worker/thread limits in mpm_event/mpm_worker config — may be exhausted under load

regex: AH02572.*certificate.*does not match server name|SSL.*hostname mismatch
title: Apache SSL certificate hostname mismatch
fixes: Ensure the certificate's CN or SAN matches the ServerName in the VirtualHost
Use a certificate with Subject Alternative Names covering all domains
Check SNI configuration — each SSL VirtualHost needs its own ServerName and matching cert

regex: worker_connections are not enough|worker_connections exceed open file
title: Nginx worker connections limit reached
fixes: Increase worker_connections in events block: worker_connections 4096
Also increase system open file limit: ulimit -n 65536 and worker_rlimit_nofile directive
Tune worker_processes to auto or match CPU cores

title: Nginx connection reset by upstream
fixes: Enable proxy_next_upstream to retry on another server: proxy_next_upstream error timeout http_502
Investigate backend stability — it's forcibly closing connections
Increase keepalive_timeout on both nginx and backend; check for backend connection limits

title: Nginx SSL handshake failure with upstream
fixes: Ensure proxy_ssl_protocols and proxy_ssl_ciphers are compatible with the upstream
For self-signed upstream certs: proxy_ssl_verify off or provide proxy_ssl_trusted_certificate
Check that the upstream server supports the TLS version nginx is offering

regex: upstream sent too big header|upstream sent invalid header
title: Nginx upstream response header too large
fixes: Increase proxy_buffer_size: proxy_buffer_size 16k (handles large headers/cookies)
Also increase proxy_buffers: proxy_buffers 4 32k
Increase large_client_header_buffers for large request headers: large_client_header_buffers 4 16k

title: Apache configuration syntax error
fixes: Run apachectl configtest to see the exact line and error
Check for missing closing tags (</Directory>, </VirtualHost>)
Verify all loaded modules are installed: apachectl -M to list active modules

title: Apache reverse proxy permission denied
fixes: Enable mod_proxy and mod_proxy_http: a2enmod proxy proxy_http
Add <Proxy *> Require all granted </Proxy> for the proxy target
Check ProxyPass and ProxyPassReverse URLs are correct and backend is reachable

title: SSL/TLS certificate expired
fixes: Renew the certificate immediately — use certbot renew for Let's Encrypt or request from your CA
Set up auto-renewal cron job: 0 0 1 * * certbot renew --post-hook 'systemctl reload nginx'
Check all servers/load balancers serving this domain to update the cert everywhere

regex: self.signed certificate|DEPTH_ZERO_SELF_SIGNED_CERT|unable to verify the first certificate
title: Self-signed certificate rejected
fixes: Replace with a certificate from a trusted CA (Let's Encrypt is free)
For internal services, add the self-signed CA to the client's trust store
Include the full certificate chain (intermediate certs) in the server's certificate file

title: HSTS forcing HTTPS causing access issues
fixes: Clear the HSTS cache in browser: chrome://net-internals/#hsts → delete domain
Ensure HTTPS is fully working before enabling HSTS — once set, browsers won't use HTTP
Start with a short max-age (300) for testing, then increase to 31536000 for production

regex: Mixed Content.*was loaded over HTTPS.*requested an insecure|blocked.*mixed.*content
title: Mixed content - HTTP resource on HTTPS page
fixes: Change all resource URLs to HTTPS or use protocol-relative //domain.com/path
Add Content-Security-Policy: upgrade-insecure-requests header to auto-upgrade HTTP to HTTPS
Audit all script/link/img/iframe sources and hardcoded URLs in the codebase

title: Content Security Policy violation blocking resource
fixes: Add the blocked resource's origin to the appropriate CSP directive (script-src, style-src, img-src, etc.)
Use nonce-based CSP for inline scripts: script-src 'nonce-{random}' and add nonce attribute to script tags
Check the browser console for the exact directive that blocked the resource and update accordingly

title: SameSite cookie attribute blocking cross-site requests
fixes: Set SameSite=None; Secure on cookies that need cross-site access (requires HTTPS)
For same-site only cookies, use SameSite=Lax (default in modern browsers) or SameSite=Strict
Update your session/auth library to set the correct SameSite attribute on all auth cookies

title: Certificate authority not trusted by client
fixes: Include intermediate certificates in the server's certificate chain file
Verify the chain order: server cert first, then intermediates, root last (or omit root)
Test with: openssl s_client -connect host:443 -servername host — check 'Verify return code'

title: SSL/TLS cipher or protocol version mismatch
fixes: Update cipher suite to include modern ciphers: ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:...'
Enable TLS 1.2 and 1.3: ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3 (disable SSLv3, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1)
Check if client supports any of the server's offered ciphers — update one or both sides

regex: UNABLE_TO_GET_ISSUER_CERT_LOCALLY|unable to get local issuer certificate
title: SSL client cannot find issuer certificate locally
fixes: Install the CA certificate bundle: apt install ca-certificates && update-ca-certificates
Set the CA bundle path in your client: NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS=/path/to/ca-bundle.crt
For Node.js, pass ca option to https.request() with the custom CA certificate

title: SSL certificate hostname doesn't match
fixes: Get a new certificate with the correct hostname in the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) field
If using a wildcard cert (*.domain.com), it only covers one subdomain level — not sub.sub.domain.com
Check the actual hostname being connected to — it must exactly match a SAN entry on the cert

title: OCSP stapling failure
fixes: Ensure the server can reach the CA's OCSP responder URL (check firewall/DNS)
Add resolver directive in nginx for OCSP: resolver 8.8.8.8 valid=300s
Verify the OCSP responder URL in the cert: openssl x509 -noout -ocsp_uri -in cert.pem

title: SSL certificate verification failed
fixes: Check the full certificate chain is present and in correct order
Ensure the CA root certificate is in the trust store of the verifying system
Verify the cert hasn't been revoked: check CRL or OCSP status

title: X-Frame-Options blocking iframe embedding
fixes: Set X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN to allow same-domain framing
Use Content-Security-Policy: frame-ancestors 'self' https://trusted.com for more control
Remove X-Frame-Options header if the page is intentionally embeddable (and use CSP frame-ancestors instead)

title: Referrer-Policy stripping referrer information
fixes: Set Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin for a balance of privacy and functionality
Use no-referrer-when-downgrade if you need the full URL sent to same-protocol destinations
For analytics that depend on referrer, ensure the policy permits it for same-origin requests

title: SSL protocol error - likely HTTP on HTTPS port
fixes: Ensure the server is actually configured for SSL on the port — not serving plain HTTP on 443
Check that ssl_certificate and ssl_certificate_key are configured in the server block for port 443
Verify the client is connecting with HTTPS (not HTTP) to the SSL-enabled port

title: Permissions-Policy blocking browser feature
fixes: Add the required feature to Permissions-Policy header: Permissions-Policy: camera=self, microphone=self
For iframes, add allow attribute: <iframe allow='camera; microphone'>
Check which features are blocked in DevTools Console and add them to the policy as needed

title: Certificate Transparency requirement not met
fixes: Ensure your CA provides SCTs (Signed Certificate Timestamps) embedded in the certificate
Use a CA that supports Certificate Transparency (most public CAs do since 2018)
Check if the TLS extension for CT is being served via OCSP stapling or TLS extension

title: CORS with credentials cannot use wildcard origin
fixes: Replace Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * with the specific requesting origin when credentials are used
Read the Origin header from the request and echo it back (after validating it's allowed)
Set Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true only with a specific, validated origin

title: Subresource Integrity hash mismatch
fixes: Regenerate the SRI hash: shasum -b -a 384 file.js | xxd -r -p | base64
The file content may have changed on the CDN — update the integrity attribute with the new hash
Ensure the file isn't being modified by a CDN optimization (minification, compression) after hash was computed

title: HTTP Public Key Pinning validation failure
fixes: HPKP is deprecated — remove Public-Key-Pins header from your server configuration
If still using HPKP, add a backup pin and ensure the pinned key matches current or backup cert
Clear the pin in browser by visiting the domain after removing the header (respect max-age expiry)

title: WebSocket closed abnormally (code 1006)
fixes: 1006 means no close frame was received — check for network interruptions or proxy timeouts
Implement automatic reconnection with exponential backoff on abnormal close
Ensure intermediary proxies (nginx, ALB) have WebSocket timeout set high enough: proxy_read_timeout 3600s

regex: WebSocket.*handshake.*failed|Error during WebSocket handshake|unexpected response code: 4\d{2}
title: WebSocket handshake failure
fixes: Ensure the server responds with HTTP 101 Switching Protocols — check for middleware intercepting the upgrade
Verify proxy is configured for WebSocket: proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'
Check that the Origin header is allowed by the server's CORS/origin validation

regex: WebSocket.*frame.*too (big|large)|RangeError.*Max payload size exceeded|message too big
title: WebSocket message size limit exceeded
fixes: Increase maxPayload on the server: new WebSocket.Server({ maxPayload: 10 * 1024 * 1024 })
Implement message chunking for large payloads — split into smaller frames
Compress messages using permessage-deflate extension to reduce frame size

title: WebSocket ping/pong timeout - connection stale
fixes: Implement client-side ping: send periodic ping frames and expect pong within timeout
Server-side: use ws.isAlive flag with setInterval ping and terminate dead connections
Reduce ping interval if on unreliable networks — 30s ping with 10s pong timeout is typical

regex: WebSocket is already in CLOSING or CLOSED state
title: Attempting to send on closed WebSocket
fixes: Check ws.readyState === WebSocket.OPEN before calling ws.send()
Queue messages during reconnection and flush when connection is re-established
Add an 'open' event handler to confirm the connection is ready before sending

title: WebSocket reconnection attempts exhausted
fixes: Implement exponential backoff: delay = Math.min(baseDelay * 2^attempt, maxDelay)
Show user feedback after max retries and provide a manual reconnect button
Check if the failure is permanent (auth expired, server down) vs transient (network blip) and handle differently

title: WebSocket connection refused by server
fixes: Verify the WebSocket server is running and listening on the expected port
Check firewall rules allow inbound connections on the WebSocket port
Ensure the URL scheme is correct: ws:// for HTTP, wss:// for HTTPS

regex: Invalid frame header|RSV.*bits must be clear|invalid opcode
title: WebSocket protocol violation - invalid frame
fixes: Ensure both client and server agree on extensions (permessage-deflate RSV bits)
Check for intermediary proxies corrupting WebSocket frames — use wss:// to prevent this
Verify the WebSocket library versions are compatible and not mixing draft/final protocol versions

title: WebSocket authentication/authorization failure
fixes: Pass auth token in the connection URL query string or as a subprotocol: new WebSocket(url, [token])
Send auth in the first message after connection opens (WebSocket headers can't be customized in browsers)
Validate the token server-side in the 'upgrade' event before completing the handshake

title: WebSocket closed due to policy violation (1008)
fixes: Check the server's message validation — the message format or content violated server policy
Review rate limiting: too many messages in short time can trigger policy closes
Inspect the close reason message for specific violation details

title: WebSocket server internal error (1011)
fixes: Check server logs for the underlying error that caused the unexpected condition
Add error handling in the WebSocket message handler to prevent unhandled exceptions from closing the connection
Implement graceful error responses instead of killing the connection on server-side errors

title: WebSocket server rejected oversized message (1009)
fixes: Reduce message size or implement chunked message transfer protocol
Increase server's max message size limit if the large payload is legitimate
Compress data before sending using a library like pako/fflate for binary data

title: WebSocket connection establishment timeout
fixes: Increase connection timeout: set a setTimeout on the 'open' event and close if not received
Check network path — firewalls or corporate proxies may block WebSocket upgrade requests
Try wss:// (port 443) which is less likely to be blocked than ws:// on non-standard ports

title: WebSocket binary/text frame type mismatch
fixes: Check ws.binaryType setting on client: 'blob' or 'arraybuffer' for binary messages
Ensure sender uses ws.send(string) for text frames and ws.send(buffer) for binary
Negotiate message format during handshake using subprotocols

title: WebSocket received unsupported data type (1003)
fixes: Check that the message encoding matches what the server expects (UTF-8 for text frames)
Ensure binary data isn't being sent as a text frame (invalid UTF-8 will trigger 1003)
Validate message format on client side before sending

title: WebSocket server going away (1001)
fixes: Server is shutting down gracefully — implement reconnection logic
Check if a load balancer is draining the connection during deployment
Use connection draining: notify clients before shutdown so they can reconnect to another server

title: WebSocket connection limit reached
fixes: Implement connection pooling — share a single WebSocket for multiple subscriptions/channels
Increase server-side connection limits (ulimit, max file descriptors)
Use a message broker pattern where clients connect to fewer dedicated WebSocket gateways

title: WebSocket subprotocol negotiation failed
fixes: Ensure client requests a subprotocol the server supports: new WebSocket(url, ['graphql-ws'])
Server must echo back one of the requested subprotocols in the handshake response
Check for version mismatch: 'graphql-ws' vs 'subscriptions-transport-ws' (deprecated)

title: WebSocket rate limiting triggered
fixes: Implement client-side message throttling/debouncing before sending
Batch multiple small messages into fewer larger messages
Check server rate limit configuration and increase if the traffic is legitimate

title: WebSocket connection memory leak on server
fixes: Remove event listeners when connections close: ws.removeAllListeners() in 'close' handler
Clear intervals/timeouts associated with the connection in the close handler
Use a WeakMap or Set to track connections and remove on close — avoid closures retaining large objects

title: Apollo Client network error - server unreachable
fixes: Check that the GraphQL endpoint URL is correct and the server is running
Add an error link to the Apollo Client link chain: onError(({ networkError }) => { ... })
Implement retry logic with apollo-link-retry for transient network failures

regex: Cache data may be lost.*__typename|Missing field '(.*)' while writing result
title: Apollo cache inconsistency - missing typename or field
fixes: Ensure all queries include __typename (Apollo adds it automatically unless disabled)
Define a typePolicies merge function for the conflicting type in InMemoryCache config
Use cache.evict() or cache.modify() to clean stale cache entries causing conflicts

title: Apollo optimistic response shape doesn't match mutation result
fixes: Ensure optimisticResponse includes __typename for all object types in the response
Match the exact shape of the mutation's return type including all queried fields
Use the same field aliases in optimisticResponse as in the mutation query

title: Apollo subscription disconnected
fixes: Configure WebSocket reconnection in the subscription client: connectionParams with retry logic
Use graphql-ws library (maintained) instead of subscriptions-transport-ws (deprecated)
Handle the 'closed' event and re-subscribe when connection is re-established

title: GraphQL schema stitching type conflict
fixes: Use transforms to rename conflicting types: RenameTypes((name) => `Service_${name}`)
Define explicit type merging config in stitchSchemas for shared types
Consider using Apollo Federation instead of schema stitching for better type ownership

title: GraphQL variable type mismatch
fixes: Check that variable values match their declared types in the operation definition
Ensure enums are passed as strings without quotes in the variables object (not the query)
Convert IDs to strings and numbers to Int/Float as expected by the schema

regex: Cannot query field.*on type|Field.*doesn't exist on type
title: GraphQL field does not exist on type
fixes: Check for typos in field names — GraphQL is case-sensitive
If querying a union/interface, use inline fragments: ... on SpecificType { field }
Verify the schema is up to date — regenerate types with graphql-codegen if using code generation

regex: Store reset while query was in flight|client\.resetStore.*active queries
title: Apollo cache reset while queries are active
fixes: Use client.clearStore() instead of client.resetStore() if you don't need to refetch
Wait for active queries to complete before resetting: await Promise.all then resetStore
Call refetchQueries after resetStore to re-execute active queries with the clean cache

title: Apollo cache circular reference during serialization
fixes: Define custom cache key fields in typePolicies to prevent circular normalization
Use cache.evict() to remove circular references before extracting cache state
Check that your schema doesn't have unbounded recursive types being eagerly cached

title: GraphQL query syntax error
fixes: Validate queries at build time using graphql-eslint or graphql-codegen
Check for missing closing braces, invalid field names, or incorrect fragment syntax
Use a GraphQL IDE (GraphiQL, Apollo Studio) to test queries before embedding in code

regex: Invariant Violation.*Expecting a parsed GraphQL document|not a valid GraphQL document
title: Apollo expecting parsed document but got raw string
fixes: Wrap your query string in the gql template tag: import { gql } from '@apollo/client'
If using .graphql files, configure webpack/vite graphql-tag/loader
Ensure the query is defined with gql`...` not as a plain string

title: Apollo fetchMore pagination cache merge failing
fixes: Define a typePolicies merge function for the paginated field: merge(existing, incoming, { args })
Use the keyArgs option to specify which arguments identify unique cache entries
Return the merged array correctly: [...(existing?.items || []), ...incoming.items]

title: GraphQL subscription error not handled
fixes: Add an error callback to the subscription: subscribe({ next, error: (err) => handle(err) })
Handle errors in the subscription link: new GraphQLWsLink with on: { error: handler }
Differentiate between connection errors and subscription payload errors in the handler

title: GraphQL query complexity/depth limit exceeded
fixes: Reduce query depth by splitting into multiple simpler queries
Request pagination with smaller page sizes to reduce response complexity
Ask the API team to increase the complexity limit or add the query to an allowlist

regex: Cannot return null for non-nullable field|null.*non-null
title: GraphQL non-nullable field returned null
fixes: Fix the resolver to always return a value for non-nullable fields
If null is a valid state, change the schema to make the field nullable: field: String → field: String!
Check for data inconsistencies in the database that leave required fields empty

title: Apollo persisted query hash not found on server
fixes: Enable Automatic Persisted Queries (APQ): server will request the full query on first miss
Ensure the client is configured with createPersistedQueryLink() in the link chain
If using a query allowlist, register the query hash with the server before deployment

title: GraphQL server doesn't support persisted queries
fixes: Disable APQ on the client: remove persistedQueryLink from the Apollo link chain
Enable APQ on the server: new ApolloServer({ persistedQueries: { cache } })
Fall back to full query: the link should automatically retry with the full document

title: Apollo query callback fires after component unmount
fixes: Use the cleanup return from useEffect to cancel or ignore stale results
Check if the component is still mounted before updating state in onCompleted callback
Use AbortController with apollo-link-http to cancel in-flight requests on unmount

title: Apollo received non-200 HTTP status from server
fixes: Check the server logs for the actual error — the response body usually contains GraphQL error details
Add an HTTP link error handler to parse and display the error message
Verify authentication headers are being sent correctly (token expired, missing Authorization header)

title: GraphQL fragment type mismatch or duplication
fixes: Ensure the fragment's type condition matches the field's type: ...FragName on CorrectType
Use inline fragments for union types: ... on TypeA { fieldA } ... on TypeB { fieldB }
Remove duplicate fragment definitions — each fragment name must be unique per document

title: Prisma P2002 - unique constraint violation
fixes: Use upsert() instead of create() for records that might already exist
Catch the P2002 error and return a user-friendly 'already exists' message
Check for race conditions in concurrent creates — use a transaction with serializable isolation

regex: P2003.*Foreign key constraint failed on the field.*`(.*)`
title: Prisma P2003 - foreign key constraint violation
fixes: Ensure the referenced record exists before creating the child record
Use connect syntax to link existing records: create({ data: { relation: { connect: { id } } } })
Check for cascade delete issues — the parent may have been deleted before this operation

regex: P2025.*Record to (update|delete) not found|An operation failed because.*depends on.*record.*not found
title: Prisma P2025 - record not found for operation
fixes: Use findUnique() first to check existence, then update/delete with a guard
Use updateMany/deleteMany which return count 0 instead of throwing when no record matches
Handle P2025 in a try/catch and return a 404 response to the client

title: Prisma migration drift detected
fixes: Run prisma migrate diff to see what drifted from the expected schema
Use prisma migrate resolve --applied <migration_name> to mark migrations as already applied
Reset with prisma migrate reset (WARNING: drops data) or baseline with prisma migrate diff --from-empty

title: Prisma schema validation error
fixes: Run prisma validate to get detailed error location in schema.prisma
Check for missing relation annotations: every relation needs @relation on both sides
Ensure all model fields have valid types and every model has at least one @id or @@id

regex: P2024.*Timed out fetching a new connection from the connection pool
title: Prisma connection pool timeout
fixes: Increase pool size in the connection string: ?connection_limit=20
Reduce long-running transactions that hold connections from the pool
Use prisma.$disconnect() in serverless functions after operations to release connections

regex: P2021.*table.*does not exist in the current database
title: Prisma P2021 - table not found in database
fixes: Run prisma migrate deploy to apply pending migrations that create the table
Check DATABASE_URL points to the correct database
If the table was created outside Prisma, run prisma db pull to update the schema

regex: P2022.*column.*does not exist in the current database
title: Prisma P2022 - column not found in database
fixes: Run prisma migrate deploy to apply pending migrations that add the column
Check if the column was renamed — update the @map() annotation or create a new migration
Run prisma db pull to sync schema with the actual database state

title: Prisma cannot connect to database server
fixes: Verify the database is running and accepting connections on the specified host:port
Check DATABASE_URL format: postgresql://user:password@host:5432/dbname
For Docker, ensure the database container is on the same network and use service name as host

regex: P1008.*Operations timed out|P1017.*Server has closed the connection
title: Prisma database operation timeout or closed connection
fixes: Increase connection timeout in URL: ?connect_timeout=30
Check for database server restarts or network instability
Implement retry logic for transient connection failures in your application layer

title: Prisma P2014 - required relation violation
fixes: Ensure the operation doesn't orphan required relations — delete children first or use cascade
Set onDelete: Cascade in the @relation annotation if parent deletion should cascade
Use a transaction to delete related records before the parent

regex: P2016.*Query interpretation error|Error interpreting the query
title: Prisma query interpretation error
fixes: Check for invalid filter combinations in your where clause
Ensure relation filters use the correct nesting: where: { relation: { some: { field: value } } }
Simplify complex queries and rebuild incrementally to find the problematic filter

title: Prisma client not generated or out of date
fixes: Run npx prisma generate to regenerate the client after schema changes
Add prisma generate to your postinstall script: "postinstall": "prisma generate"
Ensure @prisma/client is in dependencies (not devDependencies) for production builds

title: Prisma migration failed - object already exists
fixes: The migration is trying to create something that already exists — mark it as applied: prisma migrate resolve --applied <name>
If the database was modified outside Prisma, baseline with prisma migrate diff
Edit the failed migration SQL to add IF NOT EXISTS clauses where appropriate

title: Prisma unknown request error
fixes: Check database server logs for the actual error — Prisma couldn't categorize it
Ensure the database version is compatible with your Prisma version
Try updating Prisma to latest: npm install @prisma/client@latest prisma@latest

title: Prisma related records not found during connect
fixes: Verify the ID or unique fields used in connect: { connect: { id: existingId } } point to real records
Use connectOrCreate instead of connect if the related record might not exist yet
Check for race conditions where the related record is deleted between validation and connect

title: Prisma interactive transactions not supported by provider
fixes: Upgrade to Prisma 4.7+ for broader interactive transaction support
Use sequential operations instead of interactive transactions for unsupported providers
For MongoDB, ensure your cluster is a replica set (transactions require replica sets)

title: Prisma feature not supported by database provider
fixes: Check Prisma docs for provider-specific feature support (e.g., fullTextSearch requires PostgreSQL)
Add the preview feature flag in schema.prisma: generator client { previewFeatures = ["feature"] }
Use raw queries with prisma.$queryRaw for provider-specific features not supported by Prisma's query API

title: Prisma database seed script failure
fixes: Check the seed script path in package.json: "prisma": { "seed": "ts-node prisma/seed.ts" }
For TypeScript seeds, ensure ts-node is installed and tsconfig allows the seed file
Add error handling in the seed script and use prisma.$disconnect() in a finally block

title: Prisma query engine binary not found
fixes: Run prisma generate to download the correct engine binary for your platform
Set binaryTargets in schema.prisma: generator client { binaryTargets = ["native", "linux-musl-openssl-3.0.x"] }
For Docker, add the platform target matching the container OS: "debian-openssl-3.0.x" or "linux-musl"

title: Tailwind CSS class not applying to element
fixes: Check specificity — another CSS rule may be overriding. Use !important modifier: !text-red-500
Verify the class name is correct (e.g., text-grey-500 vs text-gray-500)
Ensure the element isn't inside a scope where Tailwind is excluded by prefix or content config

title: Tailwind purge/content removing needed classes
fixes: Add all template file paths to content array in tailwind.config.js: content: ['./src/**/*.{html,ts,tsx,jsx}']
Don't construct class names dynamically (e.g., `text-${color}-500`) — use complete class strings
Safelist classes that are generated dynamically: safelist: ['bg-red-500', 'bg-blue-500']

title: Tailwind dark mode classes not activating
fixes: Set darkMode strategy in config: darkMode: 'class' (manual) or 'media' (system preference)
For 'class' strategy, ensure 'dark' class is on the <html> or <body> element
Check specificity — dark: variants generate .dark .dark\:class which needs the parent .dark class

title: Tailwind custom config not loading
fixes: Verify tailwind.config.js is in the project root (or specified path in PostCSS config)
Restart the dev server after changing tailwind.config.js — changes aren't always hot-reloaded
Check for syntax errors in config: module.exports = { theme: { extend: { ... } } } — use extend to not override defaults

title: Tailwind @apply directive not working
fixes: Ensure the class being applied exists in Tailwind's utility set or is defined in the config
@apply cannot use classes with modifiers like hover: or responsive prefixes — apply the base class and add the variant separately
Check that PostCSS is processing the file — @apply only works in files processed by Tailwind's PostCSS plugin

title: Tailwind JIT mode not compiling classes
fixes: JIT is default since Tailwind v3 — ensure content paths are correct so the compiler can find classes
Restart the dev server — JIT watches files specified in content config
Check that the file extension is included in content glob: './src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,html,vue}'

title: Tailwind arbitrary value syntax not working
fixes: Ensure no spaces inside the brackets: w-[300px] not w-[ 300px ]
Escape special characters: content-['hello\_world'] for underscore-as-space
Arbitrary values require JIT mode (Tailwind v3+) — upgrade if on v2

title: Tailwind plugin registration error
fixes: Install the plugin package: npm install @tailwindcss/forms and add to config plugins array
Use the correct plugin API: plugin(({ addUtilities, theme }) => { ... })
Check plugin compatibility with your Tailwind version — some plugins require v3+

title: Tailwind CSS package not found by PostCSS
fixes: Install tailwindcss: npm install -D tailwindcss postcss autoprefixer
Ensure postcss.config.js includes tailwindcss: module.exports = { plugins: { tailwindcss: {}, autoprefixer: {} } }
Check that tailwindcss is in devDependencies and node_modules is populated (run npm install)

title: Tailwind PostCSS version incompatibility
fixes: Tailwind v3 requires PostCSS 8 — upgrade: npm install -D postcss@8 autoprefixer@latest
If stuck on PostCSS 7, use the compatibility build: npm install -D @tailwindcss/postcss7-compat
Check other PostCSS plugins for PostCSS 8 compatibility and update them too

title: Tailwind responsive breakpoint classes not working
fixes: Tailwind uses mobile-first: sm: applies at 640px AND UP — base styles are mobile, add breakpoints for larger
Add viewport meta tag: <meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1'>
Check for CSS that sets a fixed width on a parent, preventing responsive behavior

title: Tailwind group/peer modifiers not triggering
fixes: Add the 'group' class to the parent element: <div class='group'> and 'group-hover:' to child
For peer, the peer element must come BEFORE the target in the DOM (CSS ~ selector limitation)
Use named groups for nested groups: 'group/name' on parent, 'group-hover/name:' on child

title: Tailwind important modifier not taking effect
fixes: Use the ! prefix: !text-red-500 generates text-red-500 !important
Set important: true in tailwind.config.js to make ALL utilities !important
Better: set important: '#app' to scope Tailwind under a selector for higher specificity without !important

title: Tailwind animation or transition classes not working
fixes: For transitions, you need both the transition utility AND the property to transition: 'transition-colors duration-300'
Ensure the element has different states to transition between (hover:bg-blue-500 with transition)
For custom animations, define keyframes in tailwind.config.js theme.extend.keyframes and reference in animation

title: Tailwind container class not behaving as expected
fixes: Container doesn't auto-center — add mx-auto: <div class='container mx-auto'>
Configure container center in config: theme: { container: { center: true, padding: '2rem' } }
Container sets max-width per breakpoint but NOT padding — add px-4 or configure default padding

title: Tailwind color opacity modifier not working
fixes: Use the slash syntax: bg-red-500/50 for 50% opacity (requires Tailwind v3+)
For older versions, use bg-opacity-50 as a separate class
Ensure you haven't overridden the color in theme without the CSS variable format Tailwind needs for opacity

title: Tailwind Typography plugin prose class not working
fixes: Install the plugin: npm install -D @tailwindcss/typography and add to plugins in config
Add the 'prose' class to the container wrapping your HTML content
Customize with prose modifiers: prose-lg, prose-invert, or override in config theme.extend.typography

title: Tailwind space/divide utilities not affecting children
fixes: space-x/y and divide-x/y only work on direct children — they use > * + * selector
Ensure the parent has the class, not the children:
These utilities don't work with absolutely positioned or hidden children — use gap with flex/grid instead

title: Tailwind ring utility not visible
fixes: Ring needs both ring width AND color: 'ring-2 ring-blue-500' (default ring color may be transparent)
For focus rings, use: 'focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500 focus:outline-none'
Ring-offset needs ring-offset-color set and may be hidden by parent overflow:hidden

title: Tailwind @layer directive styles not applied correctly
fixes: Ensure @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities; are in your main CSS file in that order
Styles in @layer base have lower specificity than utilities — this is by design for overridability
Use @layer components for reusable component styles that should sit between base and utilities in specificity

title: Lambda Cold Start Timeout
fixes: Enable provisioned concurrency
Reduce deployment package size
Use Lambda SnapStart for Java runtimes

title: Lambda Function Timeout
fixes: Increase timeout in Lambda configuration
Optimize function code to reduce execution time
Use Step Functions for long-running workflows

title: API Gateway Integration Timeout
fixes: Increase API Gateway timeout (max 29s)
Implement async pattern with SQS
Add caching with API Gateway stage cache

title: DynamoDB Throughput Exceeded
fixes: Enable auto-scaling on the table
Switch to on-demand capacity mode
Implement exponential backoff in client code

title: S3 Access Denied
fixes: Check bucket policy allows the IAM role
Verify object ACL permissions
Ensure KMS key policy grants decrypt access

title: CloudFormation Stack Rollback
fixes: Check CloudFormation events for root cause
Fix the failing resource and redeploy
Use change sets to preview before deploying

title: ECS Essential Container Failure
fixes: Check container logs in CloudWatch
Verify health check configuration
Ensure sufficient memory/CPU allocation

title: RDS Connection Failure
fixes: Check security group inbound rules for DB port
Verify RDS instance is accessible from your VPC
Enable RDS Proxy for connection pooling

title: SQS Visibility Timeout Expired
fixes: Increase visibility timeout to match processing time
Call ChangeMessageVisibility for long-running tasks
Implement batch processing with shorter per-item time

title: SNS Delivery Failure - Endpoint Disabled
fixes: Re-enable the subscription endpoint
Implement dead-letter queue for failed deliveries
Check platform application credentials

regex: is not authorized to perform.*because no identity-based policy
title: IAM Policy Deny - No Identity Policy
fixes: Attach required IAM policy to the user/role
Use IAM Policy Simulator to debug permissions
Check for explicit deny in SCPs

title: Lambda Runtime Exit Error
fixes: Check for unhandled exceptions in handler
Verify runtime compatibility with package
Increase memory allocation to prevent OOM kills

title: API Gateway Rate Limit Exceeded
fixes: Request throttle limit increase via Support
Implement retry with exponential backoff
Add API key usage plans

title: DynamoDB Conditional Check Failed
fixes: Review condition expression logic
Implement optimistic locking with version attribute
Use TransactWriteItems for atomic operations

regex: NoSuchKey.*The specified key does not exist
title: S3 Object Not Found
fixes: Verify the object key path and bucket name
Check if object was lifecycle-expired
Enable S3 versioning to recover deleted objects

title: CloudFormation Resource Creation Failed
fixes: Read the StatusReason in stack events
Validate template with cfn validate-template
Check resource limits in the target region

title: ECS Cannot Pull Container Image
fixes: Verify ECR repository and image tag exist
Check task execution role has ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
Ensure VPC has NAT gateway or ECR endpoint

title: RDS Max Connections Exceeded
fixes: Use RDS Proxy to pool connections
Increase max_connections in parameter group
Close idle connections in application code

title: SQS Invalid Message Body
fixes: Ensure message body is valid UTF-8
Use SQS Extended Client for messages over 256KB
Base64 encode binary data before sending

title: IAM Access Denied Exception
fixes: Check for explicit Deny statements in policies
Verify resource-based policy allows cross-account
Review service control policies

title: Azure App Service Deployment Failed
fixes: Check deployment logs in Kudu console
Verify app settings and connection strings
Ensure deployment slot is not locked

title: Azure Function Timeout
fixes: Increase functionTimeout in host.json
Use Durable Functions for long-running workflows
Switch to Premium plan for longer timeout

title: Cosmos DB Request Rate Too Large
fixes: Increase provisioned RU/s or enable autoscale
Implement retry with exponential backoff
Optimize queries to consume fewer RUs

title: Azure Blob Storage SAS Token Expired
fixes: Generate a new SAS token with longer validity
Use Azure AD authentication instead of SAS
Implement token refresh logic before expiry

title: Azure Key Vault Access Denied
fixes: Add access policy for the calling identity
Switch to RBAC-based access control
Verify managed identity is enabled on the resource

title: Cosmos DB Service Busy
fixes: Implement retry with SDK retry policy
Enable server-side retries in Cosmos DB
Reduce batch operation sizes

title: Azure App Service Package Deploy Error
fixes: Verify zip package is valid and accessible
Check WEBSITE_RUN_FROM_PACKAGE URL or storage
Clear deployment cache and retry

title: Azure Function Host Not Running
fixes: Check Application Insights for startup exceptions
Verify function app runtime version
Restart function app and check platform logs

regex: AuthorizationFailed.*does not have authorization to perform
title: Azure RBAC Authorization Failed
fixes: Assign the required Azure role to the identity
Check scope of role assignment
Use az role assignment list to verify

title: Cosmos DB Offer Exceeded
fixes: Scale up throughput provisioning
Enable autoscale to handle burst traffic
Partition data more evenly

regex: BlobNotFound|The specified blob does not exist
title: Azure Blob Not Found
fixes: Verify container and blob path are correct
Check if soft delete can recover the blob
Ensure storage account and container exist

title: Azure Deployment Conflict
fixes: Wait for current deployment to complete
Cancel the active deployment if stuck
Use deployment slots to avoid conflicts

title: Azure Key Vault Secret Not Found
fixes: Verify secret name spelling and case
Check if secret was soft-deleted
Ensure Key Vault reference syntax is correct

title: Azure Function Out of Memory
fixes: Upgrade to a higher App Service plan
Optimize memory usage in function code
Stream large data instead of loading into memory

title: Cosmos DB Partition Key Error
fixes: Include partition key in all queries
Verify partition key path matches container config
Use cross-partition query only when necessary

regex: SAS token does not allow access to this resource
title: Azure SAS Insufficient Permissions
fixes: Regenerate SAS with required permissions
Check srt and ss parameters in token
Use stored access policy for management

title: Azure Key Vault Network Restriction
fixes: Add client IP or VNet to Key Vault firewall
Enable service endpoint for calling subnet
Use Private Link for secure access

title: Azure App Service Build Failed
fixes: Check Oryx build logs in Kudu
Set SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT=false and build locally
Verify runtime version matches stack

title: Azure Functions Scale Controller Error
fixes: Check for binding connection string errors
Verify storage account health for Consumption plan
Review scale controller logs

title: Cosmos DB Document Too Large
fixes: Split document into smaller documents (max 2MB)
Store large blobs in Blob Storage with reference
Compress payload before storing

title: Firestore Permission Denied
fixes: Update Firestore security rules to allow the operation
Check auth state - user may not be authenticated
Verify rule conditions match document path

title: Firebase Quota Exceeded
fixes: Upgrade to Blaze plan for higher limits
Optimize reads/writes to reduce usage
Enable billing alerts and monitor usage dashboard

regex: The query requires an index.*create it here
title: Firestore Index Not Defined
fixes: Click the link in the error to auto-create index
Add composite index in firestore.indexes.json
Deploy indexes with firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes

title: Firebase Auth Token Expired
fixes: Call getIdToken(true) to force refresh
Implement onAuthStateChanged listener for token refresh
Check if user was disabled or deleted

regex: Function execution took \d+ ms, finished with status: 'timeout'
title: Cloud Functions Timeout
fixes: Increase timeoutSeconds in function options
Optimize function to complete faster
Use runWith({timeoutSeconds: 540}) for heavy tasks

regex: Failed to get document because the client is offline
title: Firestore Offline Cache Error
fixes: Enable offline persistence with enablePersistence()
Check network connectivity before operations
Use getDocFromServer() to bypass cache

title: Firebase Auth Network Error
fixes: Check internet connectivity
Verify Firebase project is not blocked by firewall
Retry with exponential backoff

title: Firestore Document Already Exists
fixes: Use set() with merge option instead of create()
Check for existing document before creating
Use transactions for atomic create-if-not-exists

title: Cloud Functions Cold Start
fixes: Set minInstances to keep warm instances
Reduce function dependencies and bundle size
Use lazy loading for heavy imports

title: Firebase Storage Permission Denied
fixes: Update Storage security rules
Verify user is authenticated before upload
Check file size against rule constraints

title: Firebase Auth Email Already In Use
fixes: Prompt user to sign in with existing account
Implement account linking for multiple providers
Check fetchSignInMethodsForEmail before creating

title: Firestore Deadline Exceeded
fixes: Reduce query complexity and result size
Add appropriate indexes for the query
Increase timeout in Firestore settings

title: Firebase Billing Not Configured
fixes: Link a billing account in Firebase console
Upgrade from Spark to Blaze plan
Set budget alerts to control spending

title: Firebase Requires Recent Login
fixes: Re-authenticate user with reauthenticateWithCredential
Prompt user to sign in again before sensitive op
Use auth.currentUser.getIdToken(true) to refresh

title: Cloud Function Missing Return
fixes: Return a Promise from the function handler
Add return statement before async operations
Use async/await and ensure function returns value

title: Firestore Service Unavailable
fixes: Enable offline persistence for resilience
Implement retry logic with connectivity check
Use enableNetwork/disableNetwork for explicit control

title: Firebase Storage Retry Limit
fixes: Check network stability before upload
Implement resumable uploads for large files
Reduce file size or compress before upload

regex: Exceeded maximum allowed number of rules
title: Firebase Rules Limit Exceeded
fixes: Refactor rules to use functions for reuse
Reduce nested conditions with helper functions
Split into multiple rule files with imports

title: Firebase Auth Popup Closed
fixes: Show user-friendly message to retry sign-in
Use signInWithRedirect as fallback
Check for popup blockers and inform user

title: Firestore Circular Reference
fixes: Remove circular references from document data
Use toJSON() or serialize objects before saving
Flatten nested objects structure

title: Vercel Build Failed
fixes: Check build logs for the failing command
Verify build command in vercel.json matches package.json
Ensure all dependencies are listed (not just devDependencies)

title: Vercel Function Size Limit Exceeded
fixes: Use dynamic imports to reduce bundle size
Exclude unnecessary dependencies with .vercelignore
Split into multiple smaller functions

title: Vercel Edge Function Crash
fixes: Check for Node.js APIs not available in Edge runtime
Use edge-compatible packages only
Add try-catch and return proper Response objects

title: Deployment Environment Variable Missing
fixes: Add variable in Vercel/Netlify project settings
Ensure variable is available for the correct environment
Check variable name matches exactly (case-sensitive)

title: Deployment Redirect Loop
fixes: Check _redirects file for conflicting rules
Verify force HTTPS is not conflicting with existing rules
Remove trailing slash redirect conflicts

title: ISR Revalidation Failed
fixes: Verify revalidation API route returns correct status
Check revalidation token/secret is configured
Ensure data source is accessible during revalidation

title: Netlify Build Command Failed
fixes: Check netlify.toml build.command configuration
Verify Node.js version in environment variables
Run build locally to reproduce the error

title: Netlify Function Bundling Error
fixes: Check included_files in netlify.toml for native modules
Use esbuild-compatible dependencies
Add external_node_modules configuration

title: Deployment Branch Not Linked
fixes: Link branch to project in dashboard settings
Verify git remote is correctly configured
Check deploy contexts in configuration file

title: Vercel Serverless Function Timeout
fixes: Optimize function to complete within time limit
Upgrade to Pro plan for 60s timeout
Use background functions for long tasks

title: Deployment Output Size Exceeded
fixes: Remove unnecessary static assets from output
Configure output file tracing to minimize size
Use CDN for large media files instead of bundling

title: Netlify Plugin Build Hook Failed
fixes: Check plugin version compatibility
Review plugin configuration in netlify.toml
Disable plugin temporarily to isolate issue

title: Vercel Middleware Error
fixes: Check middleware uses Edge Runtime compatible APIs
Verify matcher config in middleware.ts
Add error handling with NextResponse.next()

title: Deployment Build Cache Full
fixes: Clear build cache in project settings
Add cache-specific ignore paths
Reduce cached dependency size

title: Deployment Domain DNS Conflict
fixes: Remove conflicting DNS records
Verify domain ownership with TXT record
Use different subdomain or remove from other project

title: Vercel Image Optimization Error
fixes: Check image source URL is accessible
Verify remotePatterns in next.config.js
Reduce image dimensions to within limits

regex: Exceeded maximum number of deployments per day
title: Deployment Rate Limit
fixes: Wait for rate limit to reset
Batch changes into fewer deployments
Upgrade plan for higher deployment limits

title: Netlify Forms Submission Failed
fixes: Ensure form has netlify attribute in HTML
Check form name matches between HTML and handler
Verify spam filter is not blocking submissions

regex: Incremental Static Regeneration is not compatible
title: ISR Compatibility Error
fixes: Ensure hosting platform supports ISR
Use on-demand revalidation API instead
Switch to client-side fetching for dynamic data

title: Serverless Function Connection Refused
fixes: Verify API URL is accessible from serverless environment
Check environment variables for correct API endpoints
Ensure target service allows connections from platform IPs

title: npm Peer Dependency Conflict
fixes: Use --legacy-peer-deps flag to bypass
Align dependency versions to satisfy peers
Add overrides field in package.json to force resolution

regex: Cannot find module.*\.pnp\.cjs|Your application tried to access
title: Yarn Berry PnP Module Not Found
fixes: Run yarn dlx @yarnpkg/sdks to patch IDE
Add packageExtensions in .yarnrc.yml for missing deps
Use nodeLinker: node-modules if PnP incompatible

title: pnpm Peer Dependency Issues
fixes: Add shamefully-hoist=true in .npmrc
Use pnpm install --no-strict-peer-dependencies
Add peerDependencyRules.ignoreMissing in package.json

regex: Allowed memory size of \d+ bytes exhausted.*Composer
