regex: error MSB3027.*Could not copy.*being used by another process|IOException.*used by another process
title: MSB3027: File locked by another process during build
fixes: Stop the running application (dotnet run / IIS Express / kestrel)
Close any file handles — check with Process Explorer or 'handle.exe'
Delete bin/ and obj/ folders and rebuild
Disable 'Build on Save' if using an IDE extension that triggers concurrent builds

regex: connection refused|ECONNREFUSED|could not connect to server
title: Database Connection Refused
fixes: Verify the database server is running: `systemctl status postgresql` or `docker ps`
Check host and port in your connection string — default PostgreSQL is 5432, MySQL is 3306
Ensure firewall/security group allows inbound traffic on the database port

regex: statement timeout|query timeout|wait_timeout exceeded|Lock wait timeout exceeded
title: Database Query Timeout
fixes: Add indexes on columns used in WHERE, JOIN, and ORDER BY clauses
Increase timeout setting: `SET statement_timeout = '60s'` (PostgreSQL) or `SET wait_timeout=28800` (MySQL)
Optimize the query — check execution plan with EXPLAIN ANALYZE and eliminate full table scans

regex: deadlock detected|Deadlock found when trying to get lock|was deadlocked on lock resources
title: Database Deadlock Detected
fixes: Access tables in a consistent order across all transactions to prevent circular waits
Keep transactions short — move non-DB work outside the transaction block
Add retry logic with exponential backoff for deadlock-prone operations

regex: unique constraint|duplicate key value violates unique|Duplicate entry .* for key
title: Unique Constraint Violation
fixes: Use `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` (PostgreSQL) or `INSERT ... ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE` (MySQL)
Check for race conditions — add application-level deduplication before inserting
Verify your unique constraint is correct — you may need a composite unique index instead

regex: foreign key constraint|violates foreign key|Cannot add or update a child row.*foreign key
title: Foreign Key Constraint Violation
fixes: Insert the parent record before the child record — ensure referenced row exists
When deleting, use CASCADE or delete child records first
Check that the referenced column value is correct and not NULL when the FK is NOT NULL

regex: syntax error at or near|You have an error in your SQL syntax.*near
title: SQL Syntax Error
fixes: Check for missing commas, unmatched quotes, or reserved words used as identifiers (wrap in double quotes or backticks)
Verify SQL dialect — PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server have different syntax for LIMIT, string concat, etc.
Use a SQL linter or formatter to pinpoint the exact position of the error

regex: relation .* does not exist|Table .* doesn't exist|Invalid object name
title: Table or Relation Not Found
fixes: Check the schema/database context — use `SET search_path TO myschema` or fully qualify: `schema.table`
Run pending migrations: `npx prisma migrate deploy` or `alembic upgrade head`
Verify table name casing — PostgreSQL lowercases unquoted identifiers

regex: column reference .* is ambiguous|ambiguous column name
title: Ambiguous Column Reference
fixes: Prefix the column with the table name or alias: `SELECT t1.id, t2.id FROM table1 t1 JOIN table2 t2`
Use explicit column lists instead of SELECT * in JOINs
Alias columns in subqueries to avoid name collisions with outer queries

title: Too Many Database Connections
fixes: Use a connection pooler like PgBouncer (PostgreSQL) or ProxySQL (MySQL) in front of the database
Ensure your app properly closes/releases connections — check for connection leaks in error paths
Increase `max_connections` in database config or reduce pool size per application instance

title: Database Migration Failed
fixes: Check if a previous partial migration left the DB in a dirty state — manually fix and mark as applied
Run `prisma migrate resolve` or manually update the migrations table to mark the failed migration
Ensure no other process holds a lock on the migration table — check for active advisory locks

title: AWS Access Denied
fixes: Check IAM policy — use `aws sts get-caller-identity` to verify which role/user is being used
Add the required permission to the IAM policy and wait 1-2 minutes for propagation
Check for explicit Deny statements in SCPs, permission boundaries, or resource-based policies

regex: Missing region|could not determine region|Region .* not set|must specify a region
title: AWS Region Not Configured
fixes: Set the region via environment variable: `export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION=us-east-1`
Add region to your AWS config: `aws configure set region us-east-1`
Pass region explicitly in SDK client constructor: `new S3Client({ region: 'us-east-1' })`

title: AWS Credentials Not Found or Expired
fixes: Run `aws configure` to set up credentials or check `~/.aws/credentials` file exists
If using SSO, run `aws sso login --profile your-profile` to refresh the session
For EC2/Lambda, ensure the instance profile or execution role is attached correctly

regex: NoSuchBucket|The specified bucket does not exist|bucket.*not found
title: S3 Bucket Not Found
fixes: Verify the bucket name — S3 names are globally unique and case-sensitive (must be lowercase)
Check you're using the correct AWS account and region for the bucket
List your buckets with `aws s3 ls` to confirm the exact name — typos are common

regex: Task timed out after|Lambda.*timeout|execution timed out
title: AWS Lambda Timeout
fixes: Increase the timeout setting in the Lambda configuration (max 900 seconds)
Optimize cold start — reduce package size, use provisioned concurrency, or use ARM architecture
Move long-running work to Step Functions, SQS + worker, or ECS tasks instead

title: Permission Denied
fixes: Check file permissions with `ls -la` — fix with `chmod 644 file` or `chmod 755 dir`
Run with `sudo` if root access is required, or change file ownership with `chown user:group file`
For scripts, ensure the execute bit is set: `chmod +x script.sh`

title: Command Not Found
fixes: Install the missing package: `apt install <package>` or `yum install <package>`
Check if the binary is in your PATH: `echo $PATH` — add it with `export PATH=$PATH:/new/path`
For Node/Python CLIs, ensure global install: `npm install -g <pkg>` or `pip install <pkg>`

regex: No space left on device|ENOSPC|disk full
title: No Space Left on Device
fixes: Find large files: `du -sh /* | sort -rh | head -20` and clean up logs, temp files, or old Docker images
Clear package cache: `apt clean` or `docker system prune -a` to reclaim space
Check inode usage with `df -i` — you may have many small files exhausting inodes, not disk space

title: Too Many Open Files
fixes: Increase the limit: `ulimit -n 65536` for the current session
Permanently increase in `/etc/security/limits.conf`: `* soft nofile 65536` and `* hard nofile 65536`
Find the leaking process: `ls /proc/<pid>/fd | wc -l` — fix file descriptor leaks in your application

title: Segmentation Fault (Core Dumped)
fixes: Enable core dumps (`ulimit -c unlimited`) and analyze with `gdb ./program core` to find the crash location
Run with AddressSanitizer: compile with `-fsanitize=address` to detect memory errors
Check for null pointer dereference, buffer overflow, or use-after-free — common in C/C++ extensions

regex: z-index.*not working|z-index has no effect|stacking context
title: z-index Not Working
fixes: z-index only works on positioned elements — add `position: relative`, `absolute`, or `fixed`
Check for stacking context created by parent elements (opacity < 1, transform, filter, will-change)
Inspect with DevTools 3D view — the element may be in a lower stacking context regardless of z-index value

title: Flexbox Content Overflow
fixes: Add `min-width: 0` to the flex item — flex items default to `min-width: auto` which prevents shrinking below content size
For text overflow, add `overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap` on the flex child
Use `overflow: auto` on the flex container or child that should scroll instead of overflowing

title: position: sticky Not Working
fixes: Ensure a threshold is set: `top: 0` (or left/right/bottom) — sticky won't work without it
Check that no ancestor has `overflow: hidden`, `overflow: auto`, or `overflow: scroll` — this breaks sticky
The sticky element's container must be tall enough to allow scrolling — sticky only works within its parent bounds

title: @import Must Precede All Other Rules
fixes: Move all @import statements to the very top of the CSS file, before any other rules or @charset
In SCSS/Sass, use @use instead of @import — it's order-independent and avoids this issue
If using PostCSS, use `postcss-import` plugin which inlines imports at build time regardless of position

title: SCSS/Sass Variable Undefined
fixes: Ensure the file defining the variable is imported before usage: `@use 'variables' as *` or `@import 'variables'`
Check for typos in variable name — SCSS variables are case-sensitive: `$myColor` ≠ `$mycolor`
If using @use, access with namespace: `variables.$color-primary` instead of bare `$color-primary`

title: 404 Not Found — Debugging
fixes: Verify the URL path, method, and trailing slash — `/api/users` vs `/api/users/` may route differently
Check that the route is registered — look for typos in route decorator or router config
For SPAs, ensure the server has a fallback route that serves index.html for client-side routes

title: 500 Internal Server Error
fixes: Check server logs for the full stack trace — the 500 response usually hides the real error
Common causes: unhandled null reference, database connection failure, or missing environment variable
Add global error handling middleware that logs the error and returns a structured error response

title: 403 Forbidden
fixes: Check authentication token is valid and not expired — inspect the JWT payload at jwt.io
Verify the user/role has the required permission for this endpoint in your authorization logic
Check CSRF token — if using session auth, ensure the CSRF token header is included in the request

title: CORS Preflight Failure
fixes: Add CORS headers on the server: `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`, `Access-Control-Allow-Methods`, `Access-Control-Allow-Headers`
Ensure the server responds to OPTIONS preflight requests with 200/204 and the correct CORS headers
For credentials (cookies/auth), set `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` and specify the exact origin (not `*`)

title: 429 Too Many Requests (Rate Limited)
fixes: Implement exponential backoff with jitter — wait 2^attempt * random(0.5, 1.5) seconds before retrying
Check the `Retry-After` response header for how long to wait before the next request
Reduce request frequency — use caching, batch endpoints, or webhooks instead of polling

regex: Invalid hook call\. Hooks can only be called inside of the body of a function component
title: Invalid hook call
fixes: Ensure hooks are only called at the top level of a function component, not inside loops, conditions, or nested functions
Check for multiple copies of React in your bundle: run `npm ls react` and deduplicate
Verify the component is a function component, not a class component

regex: Too many re-renders\. React limits the number of renders to prevent an infinite loop
title: Too many re-renders (infinite loop)
fixes: Avoid calling setState directly in the render body — wrap in useEffect or event handler
Ensure onClick passes a function reference, not a function call: `onClick={() => fn()}` not `onClick={fn()}`
Check for useEffect with setState that triggers its own dependency

regex: Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop
title: Missing key prop in list
fixes: Add a unique `key` prop to each element returned in a .map() call
Use a stable unique identifier (id) instead of array index as key
If no id exists, combine multiple fields to create a unique key

regex: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component
title: Cannot update state on unmounted component
fixes: Return a cleanup function from useEffect to cancel async operations (e.g., AbortController)
Use a mounted ref flag: `const mounted = useRef(true)` and check before setState
Cancel subscriptions, timers, and pending requests in the useEffect cleanup

regex: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency
title: useEffect missing dependency (exhaustive-deps)
fixes: Add the missing variable to the dependency array
Wrap the dependency in useCallback or useMemo to stabilize its reference
If intentionally omitting, add `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` with a comment explaining why

regex: React Hook .+ cannot be called at the top level
title: Hook called outside component or custom hook
fixes: Move the hook call inside a function component or a custom hook (function starting with 'use')
Ensure the file exports a component or custom hook, not a plain function
Check that the function name starts with an uppercase letter (component) or 'use' (custom hook)

regex: Adjacent JSX elements must be wrapped in an enclosing tag
title: Adjacent JSX elements not wrapped
fixes: Wrap sibling elements in a parent `<div>` or `<section>`
Use a React Fragment: `<>...</>` or `<React.Fragment>...</React.Fragment>`
If returning from a map, ensure each iteration returns a single root element

regex: A component is changing an? uncontrolled input to be controlled
title: Uncontrolled to controlled input switch
fixes: Initialize state with an empty string instead of undefined: `useState('')` not `useState()`
Ensure the value prop is never undefined — use `value={val || ''}` as fallback
Decide on controlled or uncontrolled and stay consistent (use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled)

regex: A component is changing a controlled input to be uncontrolled
title: Controlled to uncontrolled input switch
fixes: Ensure the value prop never becomes undefined — provide a fallback empty string
Check that state isn't being set to undefined anywhere in your handlers
Use `value={state ?? ''}` to guard against null/undefined

regex: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match what was rendered on the server
title: React hydration mismatch
fixes: Ensure server and client render identical HTML — avoid Date.now(), Math.random() in initial render
Use useEffect for browser-only content (window, localStorage) so it only runs on client
Wrap browser-only components in a client check: `const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false)`

regex: React Hook .+ has a complex expression in the dependency array
title: Complex expression in useEffect deps
fixes: Extract the complex expression into a variable before the hook
Use useMemo to compute the derived value and pass that to the dependency array
Simplify the dependency to a primitive value that changes when the expression changes

regex: Objects are not valid as a React child
title: Object rendered as React child
fixes: Convert the object to a string: use JSON.stringify(obj) or access a specific property
If rendering a list, map the array to JSX elements instead of rendering the array directly
Check that you're not accidentally passing an object where a string/number is expected

title: Maximum update depth exceeded
fixes: Check for setState calls inside useEffect that trigger the same effect (circular update)
Ensure event handlers don't unconditionally call setState that re-triggers rendering
Add proper conditions or dependency arrays to break the update cycle

title: Cannot read properties of undefined/null
fixes: Add optional chaining: `obj?.property` instead of `obj.property`
Add a null check or default value before accessing the property
Ensure async data is loaded before rendering — use conditional rendering or loading state

regex: Rendered fewer hooks than expected|Rendered more hooks than expected
title: Hooks rendered conditionally (different count)
fixes: Never place hooks inside if/else blocks, loops, or after early returns
Move conditional logic inside the hook, not around it
Ensure all hooks are called in the same order on every render

regex: NG0100.*Expression has changed after it was checked
title: ExpressionChangedAfterItHasBeenCheckedError (NG0100)
fixes: Move the value change to ngAfterViewInit with a ChangeDetectorRef.detectChanges() call
Use setTimeout(() => { ... }) to defer the change to the next cycle
Restructure to avoid changing parent-bound values in child lifecycle hooks — use OnPush + markForCheck()

title: Circular dependency in DI (NG0200)
fixes: Break the cycle by injecting one service lazily with `@Inject(forwardRef(() => ServiceName))`
Extract shared logic into a third service that both services depend on
Use an event-based pattern (Subject) instead of direct service-to-service calls

title: NullInjectorError — missing provider
fixes: Add the service to the `providers` array of the module or component that needs it
Use `@Injectable({ providedIn: 'root' })` on the service class for app-wide singleton
If using a lazy-loaded module, ensure the service is provided in that module or root

regex: NG0303.*Can't bind to '.+' since it isn't a known property
title: Can't bind to property — not a known property (NG0303)
fixes: Import the module that declares the directive/component (e.g., FormsModule for ngModel, ReactiveFormsModule for formControl)
Check for typos in the property name or directive selector
If it's a custom component input, ensure the component is declared/exported in the correct module

title: Angular hydration mismatch (NG0500)
fixes: Add `ngSkipHydration` attribute to components that use browser-only APIs
Move DOM manipulation to afterNextRender() or afterRender() instead of ngOnInit
Ensure server-rendered HTML matches client initial render — avoid window/document in SSR paths

regex: NG8001.*'([^']+)' is not a known element
title: Unknown element — component not declared (NG8001)
fixes: Import the module that exports the component into your feature module's imports array
If standalone component, add it to the `imports` array of the consuming component
Check the component's selector matches exactly what's in the template (including 'app-' prefix)

regex: NG8002.*Can't bind to '(.+)' since it isn't a known property of '(.+)'
title: Unknown property binding (NG8002)
fixes: Import the required module (e.g., MatInputModule for matInput, RouterModule for routerLink)
Ensure the @Input() decorator is defined on the target component's property
Check for typos in the binding name — Angular bindings are case-sensitive

title: Template export not found (NG0301)
fixes: Ensure the directive is imported — e.g., FormsModule for `#f='ngForm'`
Check the exportAs property of the directive matches what you're referencing
Verify the directive/component module is in the imports of your module

title: Structural directive not available
fixes: Import CommonModule in your feature module's imports array
For standalone components, add CommonModule to the component's imports
Check that BrowserModule is only imported in AppModule — use CommonModule elsewhere

title: Angular SSR hydration node mismatch
fixes: Add `ngSkipHydration` to dynamic content containers that change between server and client
Wrap browser-only rendering in `@if (isBrowser)` using isPlatformBrowser check
Defer third-party scripts and DOM manipulations to afterNextRender()

regex: Hydration failed because the initial UI does not match|Text content does not match server-rendered HTML
title: Next.js hydration mismatch
fixes: Avoid using Date.now(), Math.random(), or browser APIs (window, localStorage) during initial render
Use `useEffect` + state to render browser-dependent content only on client
Suppress hydration warnings with `suppressHydrationWarning` on specific elements (use sparingly)

regex: ReferenceError: window is not defined|ReferenceError: document is not defined
title: window/document not defined in SSR
fixes: Wrap browser-only code in `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')` check
Use dynamic import with `{ ssr: false }`: `dynamic(() => import('./Component'), { ssr: false })`
Move browser-only logic into useEffect which only runs on client

regex: You're importing a component that needs .+\. It only works in a Client Component
title: Client component feature used in server component
fixes: Add `'use client'` directive at the top of the file that uses hooks, event handlers, or browser APIs
Extract the interactive parts into a separate client component and import it
Keep data fetching in the server component, pass data as props to client component

regex: Error: Cannot use (useState|useEffect|useRef|useContext) in a Server Component
title: React hook used in server component
fixes: Add `'use client'` at the top of the file
Split into a server component (data fetching) and client component (interactivity)
If only needed for initial data, use server-side patterns (fetch in server component, pass as props)

regex: Dynamic server usage.*couldn't be rendered statically
title: Dynamic server usage prevents static rendering
fixes: Add `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` to opt into dynamic rendering
Use `unstable_noStore()` or `noStore()` in the data-fetching function
Wrap dynamic content in Suspense boundaries to allow partial static rendering

title: Static generation / prerendering failure
fixes: Ensure all data fetches in getStaticProps/generateStaticParams handle errors gracefully
Add `fallback: 'blocking'` or `dynamicParams: true` for pages with unknown params at build time
Check that environment variables are available at build time (not just runtime)

regex: Invalid src prop.*hostname .+ is not configured under images in.*next\.config
title: Next/Image hostname not configured
fixes: Add the image hostname to `images.remotePatterns` in next.config.js
Use `images: { remotePatterns: [{ protocol: 'https', hostname: 'example.com' }] }`
For local images, import them directly: `import img from './image.png'`

regex: API resolved without sending a response.*API Route
title: API route resolved without sending response
fixes: Ensure every code path in the API handler calls `res.status().json()` or `res.end()`
Await all async operations before the handler function completes
Add `export const config = { api: { externalResolver: true } }` if handled by external middleware

regex: Bind for 0\.0\.0\.0:\d+ failed: port is already allocated
title: Port already allocated
fixes: Stop the container or process using the port: `docker ps` then `docker stop <id>`
Find what's using the port: `netstat -tlnp | grep <port>` or `lsof -i :<port>`
Map to a different host port: `-p 8081:80` instead of `-p 8080:80`

regex: pull access denied.*repository does not exist or may require.*docker login
title: Image not found / pull access denied
fixes: Check the image name and tag for typos — verify it exists on the registry
Run `docker login` to authenticate with the private registry
Ensure the image is public or your credentials have read access to the repository

regex: permission denied while trying to connect to the Docker daemon socket
title: Docker daemon permission denied
fixes: Add your user to the docker group: `sudo usermod -aG docker $USER` then re-login
Run with sudo: `sudo docker ...` (not recommended for production)
Check Docker Desktop is running (macOS/Windows)

regex: COPY failed:.*not found|file not found in build context
title: COPY failed — file not found in build context
fixes: Ensure the file path is relative to the build context (the directory passed to `docker build`)
Check .dockerignore isn't excluding the file you're trying to COPY
Verify the file exists and the path is case-sensitive (Linux containers are case-sensitive)

title: Multi-stage COPY --from failed
fixes: Ensure the stage name matches: `FROM node:18 AS builder` then `COPY --from=builder`
Check the source path exists in the builder stage after its RUN commands complete
Verify stage ordering — you can only COPY from stages defined earlier in the Dockerfile

regex: dial tcp.*connect: connection refused|Could not resolve host|network .+ not found
title: Docker network / connection issues
fixes: Use service names (not localhost) for inter-container communication in docker-compose
Ensure containers are on the same Docker network: `docker network connect <network> <container>`
Check the target service is running and listening on the expected port inside the container

regex: Error response from daemon.*Mounts denied|error while creating mount source path
title: Volume mount denied / path error
fixes: On Docker Desktop, add the path to Settings → Resources → File Sharing
Use absolute paths for volume mounts: `-v /full/path:/container/path`
On Windows, ensure the drive is shared in Docker Desktop settings and use //c/ format in Git Bash

title: Merge conflict
fixes: Open conflicted files, resolve the <<<<<<< / ======= / >>>>>>> markers, then `git add` and `git commit`
Use a merge tool: `git mergetool` (configure with `git config merge.tool <tool>`)
To abort: `git merge --abort` to return to pre-merge state

title: Detached HEAD state
fixes: Create a branch to keep your work: `git checkout -b my-branch`
Return to a branch: `git checkout main` (commits in detached HEAD may be lost without a branch)
If you already made commits, note the SHA and cherry-pick onto a branch: `git cherry-pick <sha>`

regex: \! \[rejected\].*\(fetch first\)|failed to push some refs.*Updates were rejected
title: Push rejected — remote has new commits
fixes: Pull and rebase: `git pull --rebase origin <branch>` then push again
Pull and merge: `git pull origin <branch>` then resolve conflicts and push
Force push only if you own the branch and understand the consequences: `git push --force-with-lease`

regex: CONFLICT.*could not apply|error: could not apply
title: Rebase conflict
fixes: Resolve conflicts in the listed files, then `git add .` and `git rebase --continue`
Skip the problematic commit: `git rebase --skip` (loses that commit's changes)
Abort the rebase entirely: `git rebase --abort` to return to original state

title: Stash apply conflict
fixes: Resolve the conflicts manually, then `git add .` — the stash remains in the list for safety
Drop the stash after resolving: `git stash drop stash@{0}`
Try applying to a clean branch: `git checkout -b temp-branch` then `git stash pop`

regex: Permission denied \(publickey\)|Could not read from remote repository
title: SSH permission denied
fixes: Check your SSH key is added to the agent: `ssh-add -l` — if empty, run `ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519`
Verify the key is added to your Git hosting account (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket Settings → SSH Keys)
Test connection: `ssh -T git@github.com` — if it fails, regenerate key: `ssh-keygen -t ed25519`

title: Git LFS smudge/download error
fixes: Run `git lfs install` to ensure LFS hooks are set up
Fetch LFS objects: `git lfs pull` or `git lfs fetch --all`
Check LFS server access and storage quota — authenticate with `git lfs env` to verify endpoint

regex: fatal: No url found for submodule path|Submodule.*registered for path.*not found
title: Submodule initialization failure
fixes: Initialize submodules: `git submodule init && git submodule update --recursive`
Re-clone with submodules: `git clone --recurse-submodules <url>`
If URL changed, update .gitmodules then run `git submodule sync && git submodule update --init`

regex: fatal: Need to specify how to reconcile divergent branches
title: Divergent branches — no reconciliation strategy
fixes: Set default pull strategy: `git config pull.rebase true` (rebase) or `git config pull.ff only` (fast-forward only)
One-time fix: `git pull --rebase origin <branch>` or `git pull --no-rebase origin <branch>`
Set globally: `git config --global pull.rebase true`

regex: fatal: bad default revision 'HEAD'|reflog.*not found|HEAD is now at
title: Lost commits — reflog recovery
fixes: Find the lost commit SHA: `git reflog` shows all recent HEAD movements
Restore with: `git checkout <sha>` then `git checkout -b recovery-branch`
Reset to it: `git reset --hard <sha>` (if on the correct branch and sure about losing current state)

title: Cannot read property of null/undefined
fixes: Add optional chaining (?.) before the property access
Add a null check before accessing the property
Ensure the variable is initialized before use
Check if an async operation has completed before accessing the result

title: Value is not a function
fixes: Check that the variable holds a function and not undefined/null
Verify the import path and that the function is exported correctly
Ensure you're not shadowing the function with a variable of the same name
Check for circular dependencies that may cause the import to be undefined

title: Assignment to constant variable
fixes: Change `const` to `let` if the variable needs reassignment
Use a new variable instead of reassigning the constant
For objects/arrays declared with const, mutate properties instead of reassigning

title: Value is not iterable
fixes: Ensure the value is an array or iterable before using for...of or spread
Provide a default empty array: `value || []`
Check that the API response returns an array, not an object or null
Use Array.from() to convert array-like objects to arrays

title: Cannot set property of null/undefined
fixes: Initialize the object before setting properties on it
Add a null check before the assignment
Ensure DOM element exists before setting attributes (check querySelector result)

title: Cannot convert undefined/null to object
fixes: Provide a fallback value: `Object.keys(value || {})`
Check for null/undefined before passing to Object.keys, Object.assign, etc.
Ensure the data source returns an object, not null

title: Variable is not defined
fixes: Import or declare the variable before using it
Check for typos in the variable name
For browser globals (window, document), ensure code runs in the correct environment
Add the missing dependency to package.json and install it

title: Cannot access variable before initialization (TDZ)
fixes: Move the variable declaration before its first usage
Check for circular dependencies between modules
Replace `let`/`const` with function declarations if hoisting is needed

title: Unexpected token
fixes: Check for missing commas, brackets, or parentheses near the reported line
Ensure JSON files don't have trailing commas or comments
Verify the file is being parsed with the correct module type (ESM vs CJS)
Check that optional chaining/nullish coalescing is supported by your target

title: Invalid regular expression
fixes: Escape special regex characters: . * + ? ^ $ { } ( ) | [ ] \
When using `new RegExp()`, double-escape backslashes: `\\d` instead of `\d`
Check for unbalanced parentheses or brackets in the pattern

title: JSON parse error
fixes: Validate the JSON string with a linter or JSON.parse in try/catch
Check for trailing commas, single quotes, or unquoted keys
Ensure the response is actually JSON and not HTML (check Content-Type header)
Look for control characters or BOM in the input string

regex: SyntaxError:.*Unexpected.*'export'|Cannot use import statement outside a module
title: Cannot use import/export outside a module
fixes: Add `"type": "module"` to package.json for ESM
Rename file to .mjs for ESM or .cjs for CommonJS
Configure your bundler/transpiler to handle ESM syntax
Use `require()` instead if targeting CommonJS

title: Maximum call stack size exceeded (infinite recursion)
fixes: Add a base case to your recursive function to stop recursion
Check for circular references in objects being serialized (JSON.stringify)
Convert recursion to iteration using a stack/queue
Check for infinite component re-renders (React useEffect deps, Angular change detection)

title: Invalid array length
fixes: Ensure the array length is a non-negative integer
Check that the value passed to `new Array()` is not negative or NaN
Validate input before using it as an array size

title: Invalid Date
fixes: Validate the date string format before passing to `new Date()`
Use ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD) for cross-browser compatibility
Check for null/undefined being passed as a date argument
Use a date library (date-fns, dayjs) for reliable parsing

regex: TS2322:.*Type '.*' is not assignable to type
title: TS2322: Type is not assignable
fixes: Fix the type mismatch — ensure the value matches the declared type
Add a type assertion if you're certain of the type: `value as MyType`
Widen the target type to accept the value (e.g., union type)
Check if null/undefined needs to be included in the type definition

regex: TS2339:.*Property '.*' does not exist on type
title: TS2339: Property does not exist on type
fixes: Add the property to the interface/type definition
Use optional chaining if the property may not exist: `obj?.prop`
Cast to a more specific type that includes the property
Check for typos in the property name

regex: TS2345:.*Argument of type '.*' is not assignable to parameter of type
title: TS2345: Argument type mismatch
fixes: Ensure the argument matches the expected parameter type
Add a type assertion: `value as ExpectedType`
Update the function signature to accept the broader type
Transform the data to match the expected shape before passing

title: TS2532: Object is possibly undefined
fixes: Add a null/undefined check before accessing the object
Use optional chaining: `obj?.property`
Use non-null assertion if you're certain it's defined: `obj!.property`
Provide a default value with nullish coalescing: `obj ?? defaultValue`

title: TS2554: Wrong number of arguments
fixes: Pass the correct number of arguments to the function
Make parameters optional with `?` if they're not always needed
Add default values to parameters: `param = defaultValue`
Check the function signature — it may have changed

title: TS2769: No overload matches this call
fixes: Check which overload signatures exist and match your arguments to one
Cast arguments to the expected types for the correct overload
Ensure you're not passing extra or incompatible arguments
Check the library's type definitions for the correct usage

title: TS7006: Parameter implicitly has any type
fixes: Add an explicit type annotation to the parameter
Set `"noImplicitAny": false` in tsconfig.json (not recommended)
Define an interface for the parameter shape

title: TS18046: Value is of type unknown
fixes: Narrow the type with a type guard: `if (value instanceof Error)`
Use a type assertion after validation: `(value as MyType)`
Add a typeof check: `if (typeof value === 'string')`

regex: TS2307:.*Cannot find module '.*' or its corresponding type declarations
title: TS2307: Cannot find module or type declarations
fixes: Install the package: `npm install <package>`
Install type declarations: `npm install -D @types/<package>`
Create a declaration file (.d.ts) with `declare module '<package>'`
Check `tsconfig.json` paths and baseUrl configuration

title: TS2531: Object is possibly null
fixes: Add a null check: `if (obj !== null)`
Use non-null assertion operator: `obj!` (only when certain)
Use optional chaining: `obj?.method()`
Provide a fallback with nullish coalescing: `obj ?? default`

regex: TS2355:.*A function whose declared type is neither 'void' nor 'any' must return a value
title: TS2355: Function must return a value
fixes: Add a return statement for all code paths
Change the return type to include `undefined` or `void` if appropriate
Add return statements to else branches and after switch cases

regex: TS2740:.*Type '.*' is missing the following properties from type
title: TS2740: Type is missing required properties
fixes: Add the missing properties to the object literal
Make the properties optional in the interface with `?`
Use `Partial<Type>` if all properties should be optional
Check if you're using the correct interface for the context

title: Module not found
fixes: Run `npm install` to install missing dependencies
Check the import path for typos and correct casing
Verify the package is listed in package.json dependencies
Check that the module's `main`/`exports` field points to the correct file

title: Cannot require() an ES Module
fixes: Use dynamic `import()` instead of `require()`
Downgrade to an older version of the package that supports CJS
Convert your project to ESM by adding `"type": "module"` to package.json
Use a bundler that handles ESM/CJS interop

title: Unhandled promise rejection
fixes: Add `.catch()` to the promise chain
Wrap async code in try/catch
Add a global handler: `process.on('unhandledRejection', handler)`
Check that all async functions are awaited

regex: TypeError:.*\.then is not a function|is not a (thenable|promise)
title: Value is not a promise/thenable
fixes: Ensure the function returns a Promise (add `async` or return `new Promise()`)
Check that you're not calling .then() on a synchronous return value
Wrap the value in `Promise.resolve(value)` if needed

title: document is not defined (SSR/Node)
fixes: Guard DOM access with `if (typeof document !== 'undefined')`
Move DOM manipulation to lifecycle hooks that only run in the browser (ngAfterViewInit, useEffect)
Use `isPlatformBrowser()` in Angular or equivalent framework check
Use JSDOM or happy-dom for testing environments

title: window is not defined (SSR/Node)
fixes: Guard with `if (typeof window !== 'undefined')`
Use dependency injection to abstract window access
Move browser-only code to client-side lifecycle hooks
Mock window in tests: `global.window = {}`

title: ENOENT: File or directory not found
fixes: Check the file path for typos and ensure it exists
Use path.resolve() or path.join() for cross-platform paths
Create the directory first with `fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })`
Check that the working directory is correct

title: EACCES: Permission denied
fixes: Fix folder permissions: `chmod -R 755 <directory>` or take ownership on Windows
Avoid running npm with sudo — fix npm prefix: `npm config set prefix ~/.npm-global`
Run the terminal as Administrator on Windows
Check if the file is locked by another process

title: EPERM: Operation not permitted
fixes: Close applications that may have the file open (editors, dev servers)
Run terminal as Administrator (Windows) or with sudo (Unix)
Delete node_modules and reinstall: `rm -rf node_modules && npm install`
Disable antivirus temporarily if it's blocking file operations

title: EADDRINUSE: Port already in use
fixes: Kill the process using the port: `npx kill-port <port>` or `lsof -ti:<port> | xargs kill`
Use a different port via environment variable or CLI flag
On Windows: `netstat -ano | findstr :<port>` then `taskkill /PID <pid> /F`

regex: Error:.*ENOSPC|no space left on device|System limit for number of file watchers reached
title: ENOSPC: Too many file watchers or no disk space
fixes: Increase file watcher limit: `echo fs.inotify.max_user_watches=524288 | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf && sudo sysctl -p`
Free disk space — clear node_modules caches: `npm cache clean --force`
Exclude node_modules from file watching in your dev tool config

regex: Module build failed.*loader|Module parse failed|You may need an appropriate loader
title: Webpack: Module parse/build failed — missing loader
fixes: Install the required loader (e.g., `npm install -D css-loader style-loader`)
Add a rule in webpack.config.js for the file type
Check that the file extension matches a configured module rule
Ensure the loader version is compatible with your webpack version

regex: error TS6059:.*is not under 'rootDir'|TS6305:.*Output file.*has not been built from source
title: TypeScript rootDir/project reference error
fixes: Ensure all source files are under the `rootDir` specified in tsconfig.json
Update `include`/`exclude` patterns in tsconfig.json
Build referenced projects first with `tsc --build`
Move the file under the correct source directory

title: Vite: Failed to resolve import
fixes: Install the missing dependency: `npm install <package>`
Add the path alias to `resolve.alias` in vite.config.ts
Check that the file extension is included if non-standard
Ensure the package has an ESM export in its package.json `exports` field

title: esbuild build failure
fixes: Check the specific error code and file/line reference in the output
Ensure syntax is compatible with the `target` setting in esbuild config
Add problematic packages to the `external` list if they shouldn't be bundled
Install missing dependencies referenced in the error

regex: Chunk \w+ \[\w+\] exceeds.*limit|entrypoint size limit|asset size limit
title: Webpack/build: Bundle size exceeds limit
fixes: Enable code splitting with dynamic imports: `import('./module')`
Analyze the bundle with `webpack-bundle-analyzer` to find large dependencies
Use tree-shaking-friendly imports: `import { x } from 'lib'` instead of `import lib`
Increase the limit in config or set `performance.hints` to 'warning'

title: Array method called on non-array
fixes: Ensure the value is an array before calling array methods
Provide a default empty array: `(data || []).map(...)`
For reduce on empty array, always provide an initial value as second argument
Check the API response shape — it may wrap the array in an object

title: Modern method not available (polyfill needed)
fixes: Check your Node.js version — upgrade to a version supporting the method
Add a polyfill via core-js: `import 'core-js/features/array/flat'`
Update the `target`/`lib` in tsconfig.json to include ES2019+
Use an alternative: `[].concat(...arr)` instead of `arr.flat()`

title: TS2304: Cannot find name
fixes: Import the type/variable from its module
Install type declarations: `npm install -D @types/node` or `@types/<lib>`
Add to `compilerOptions.types` in tsconfig.json
Declare the global in a .d.ts file: `declare const myGlobal: MyType`

regex: TS2688:.*Cannot find type definition file for
title: TS2688: Cannot find type definition file
fixes: Install the missing @types package: `npm install -D @types/<name>`
Remove the package from `types` array in tsconfig.json if not needed
Check that node_modules/@types/<name> exists and is not corrupted
Run `npm install` to restore missing packages

title: Circular dependency detected
fixes: Extract shared code into a third module that both modules can import
Use dependency injection or inversion of control to break the cycle
Move the import inside the function that needs it (lazy import)
Refactor barrel files (index.ts) — they often create accidental cycles

title: ENAMETOOLONG: File path too long
fixes: Shorten the file path or move the project closer to the root directory
On Windows, enable long paths: `git config --system core.longpaths true`
Flatten deeply nested node_modules with `npm dedupe`
Use pnpm or Yarn PnP which handle deep paths better

regex: heap out of memory|FATAL ERROR:.*allocation failed|JavaScript heap out of memory
title: JavaScript heap out of memory
fixes: Increase Node.js memory: `node --max-old-space-size=8192`
Set via env: `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`
Find and fix memory leaks — check for unbounded caches, growing arrays, or event listeners
Process large data in streams/chunks instead of loading everything into memory

regex: TypeError: unsupported operand type\(s\) for (.+): '(.+)' and '(.+)'
title: Unsupported operand types
fixes: Cast operands to compatible types (e.g., int(x) + int(y))
Check that variables hold the expected types before the operation
Use str() to concatenate strings with numbers: str(num) + text

title: Object is not callable
fixes: Remove parentheses if you're accessing a property, not calling a function
Check if you accidentally shadowed a built-in function (e.g., list = [1,2])
Ensure the variable hasn't been reassigned from a function to a value

title: Unhashable type used as dict key or in set
fixes: Convert list to tuple before using as a dict key or set element
Use frozenset() instead of set() for hashable set keys
Check that you're not accidentally passing a mutable object where an immutable one is needed

title: Wrong type passed to function
fixes: Cast the argument to the expected type (e.g., int(), str(), bytes())
Check function signature for expected parameter types
Verify the variable hasn't been reassigned to a different type upstream

regex: TypeError: (.+) takes (\d+) positional arguments? but (\d+) (?:was|were) given
title: Wrong number of arguments
fixes: Check function definition for the correct number of parameters
Add 'self' as the first parameter if this is an instance method
Use *args or **kwargs if the function should accept variable arguments

title: Cannot unpack non-iterable object
fixes: Ensure the function returns a tuple/list before unpacking (a, b = func())
Check that the right-hand side is iterable
Verify the function doesn't return None in some code paths

title: Object has no attribute
fixes: Check for typos in the attribute/method name
Verify the object is the type you expect (print(type(obj)))
Ensure the attribute is defined in __init__ or at class level

title: NoneType has no attribute
fixes: A function returned None unexpectedly — check return statements
Add a None check before accessing the attribute: if obj is not None
Verify assignments — methods like list.sort() and list.append() return None

title: Module not found
fixes: Install the package: pip install <module_name>
Activate your virtual environment before running
Check for typos in the import statement or verify the package name on PyPI

title: Cannot import name from module
fixes: Check for circular imports between modules
Verify the name exists in the target module (may have been renamed or removed)
Update the package — the name may have moved in a newer version

title: Unexpected indent
fixes: Remove extra spaces/tabs at the beginning of the line
Ensure consistent indentation (all spaces or all tabs, not mixed)
Configure your editor to use 4 spaces per indent level

title: Expected an indented block
fixes: Add a body to the if/for/def/class block (use 'pass' as placeholder)
Check that the line after a colon is properly indented
Remove empty blocks or add implementation

title: Invalid syntax
fixes: Check the line above for a missing closing parenthesis, bracket, or quote
Ensure colons are present after if/for/while/def/class statements
Verify you're not using Python 3 syntax in a Python 2 environment or vice versa

title: Unexpected end of file
fixes: Add a missing closing parenthesis, bracket, or brace
Ensure multi-line strings are properly closed with matching quotes
Check for incomplete function/class definitions at end of file

title: f-string syntax error
fixes: Escape braces in f-strings with double braces: {{ and }}
Don't use backslashes inside f-string expressions — assign to a variable first
Ensure Python version is 3.6+ for f-string support

regex: ValueError: invalid literal for int\(\) with base (\d+): '(.+)'
title: Invalid literal for int()
fixes: Strip whitespace before conversion: int(value.strip())
Validate the string contains only digits before converting
Use try/except ValueError to handle non-numeric input gracefully

regex: ValueError: too many values to unpack \(expected (\d+)\)
title: Too many values to unpack
fixes: Add more variables on the left side to match the iterable length
Use *rest to capture extra values: a, b, *rest = iterable
Check that the iterable has the expected number of elements

regex: ValueError: not enough values to unpack \(expected (\d+), got (\d+)\)
title: Not enough values to unpack
fixes: Reduce the number of variables on the left side
Verify the iterable contains enough elements
Provide default values or handle short iterables with a length check

title: Dictionary key not found
fixes: Use dict.get(key, default) to provide a fallback value
Check key existence with 'if key in dict' before accessing
Use collections.defaultdict to auto-initialize missing keys

regex: IndexError: list index out of range
title: List index out of range
fixes: Check list length before accessing: if len(lst) > index
Use try/except IndexError for safe access
Verify loop boundaries — off-by-one errors are common

regex: FileNotFoundError: \[Errno 2\] No such file or directory: '(.+)'
title: File or directory not found
fixes: Use absolute paths or verify working directory with os.getcwd()
Check file path for typos and correct OS path separators
Use pathlib.Path and .exists() to verify before opening

title: Name is not defined
fixes: Check for typos in the variable or function name
Ensure the variable is defined before it's used (order matters)
Add the missing import statement for external names

title: Maximum recursion depth exceeded
fixes: Add or fix the base case in your recursive function
Convert to an iterative approach using a stack/queue
Increase limit with sys.setrecursionlimit() only as a last resort

title: Permission denied
fixes: Run with elevated privileges (sudo on Linux/macOS, admin on Windows)
Check file permissions: os.chmod() or fix in file explorer
Ensure the file isn't locked by another process

title: Address already in use
fixes: Kill the process using the port: lsof -i :PORT then kill -9 PID
Use a different port for your server
Set SO_REUSEADDR on the socket before binding

regex: ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement (.+)
title: pip cannot find package version
fixes: Check the package name spelling on PyPI (pypi.org)
Upgrade pip: pip install --upgrade pip
Specify a valid version: pip install package==x.y.z

title: Externally managed environment (PEP 668)
fixes: Create a virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
Use pipx for CLI tools: pipx install <package>
Use --break-system-packages flag only if you understand the risks

title: Django: no such table
fixes: Run migrations: python manage.py makemigrations && python manage.py migrate
Ensure the app is in INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py
Delete db.sqlite3 and re-run migrations if the schema is corrupted

title: Django: template not found
fixes: Add the template directory to TEMPLATES[0]['DIRS'] in settings.py
Ensure APP_DIRS is True and the app is in INSTALLED_APPS
Check template file path matches the name in render() exactly

title: Django/Flask: CSRF verification failed
fixes: Add {% csrf_token %} inside your <form> tag in Django templates
Include X-CSRFToken header in AJAX requests
Ensure django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware is in MIDDLEWARE

title: Flask/Jinja2: template not found
fixes: Place templates in a 'templates/' folder relative to your app
Set template_folder parameter in Flask(__name__, template_folder='...')
Check the template filename for typos and correct case

regex: RuntimeError: Working outside of application context
title: Flask: working outside application context
fixes: Wrap code in: with app.app_context():
Use @app.before_request or access within a route handler
For CLI commands, use @app.cli.command() decorator

regex: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named '(.+)'\.?.*(?:Did you mean|\(venv\))
title: Module not found — virtual environment not activated
fixes: Activate virtual environment: source .venv/bin/activate (Linux/Mac) or .venv\Scripts\activate (Windows)
Ensure your IDE/terminal is using the correct Python interpreter from the venv
Reinstall dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt

title: Unicode decode error
fixes: Specify encoding when opening files: open(file, encoding='utf-8')
Try 'latin-1' or 'cp1252' for legacy Windows files
Use errors='ignore' or errors='replace' to skip bad bytes

title: JSON decode error
fixes: Validate JSON format — check for trailing commas, single quotes, or unquoted keys
Ensure the response is actually JSON (check Content-Type header)
Print/log the raw string before parsing to inspect malformed data

title: Requests connection error
fixes: Check that the target server is running and accessible
Verify the URL scheme (http vs https) and port number
Add retry logic with backoff: urllib3.util.retry.Retry

title: Django: database integrity constraint violation
fixes: Check for duplicate values on unique fields before saving
Use get_or_create() instead of create() to avoid duplicates
Ensure required ForeignKey fields are set before save()

regex: TypeError: Object of type (.+) is not JSON serializable
title: Object not JSON serializable
fixes: Convert datetime to string: obj.isoformat()
Use a custom encoder: json.dumps(data, default=str)
Convert sets to lists, Decimal to float before serializing

title: Asyncio timeout
fixes: Increase the timeout value in asyncio.wait_for() or aiohttp session
Add retry logic for transient network issues
Check if the awaited coroutine is blocked or deadlocked

title: StopIteration — iterator exhausted
fixes: Use next(iterator, default_value) to provide a fallback
Wrap in try/except StopIteration or use a for loop instead
Check that the iterable has elements before calling next()

title: Accessing property on undefined/null
fixes: Use optional chaining: obj?.property?.method()
Add null check: if (obj != null) { ... }
Provide default value: const items = data?.items ?? []

title: Calling a non-function value
fixes: Check the variable type before calling: typeof fn === 'function' && fn()
Verify the import/require path is correct
Ensure the method exists on the object prototype

title: File or directory not found
fixes: Verify the path exists: fs.existsSync(path)
Use path.resolve() for absolute paths
Create parent directories: fs.mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true })

title: Module resolution failure
fixes: Run npm install to ensure dependencies are installed
Check package.json for the missing dependency
Verify the import path and file extension

title: Port already in use
fixes: Kill the process using the port: lsof -ti:PORT | xargs kill
Use a different port number
Set SO_REUSEADDR option on the server socket

title: Unexpected token in parsing
fixes: Check for missing commas, brackets, or parentheses
Ensure JSON.parse() input is valid JSON
Verify the file encoding is UTF-8

title: Assignment to const variable
fixes: Use let instead of const if the value needs to change
Create a new variable for the modified value
Use object mutation instead: obj.prop = value (if const object)

title: Stack overflow from infinite recursion
fixes: Add a base case to recursive functions
Convert recursion to iteration with a loop
Check for circular references in objects

title: TypeScript type mismatch
fixes: Use a type assertion: value as ExpectedType
Fix the source type to match the target
Use a union type if multiple types are valid

title: CORS policy blocked request
fixes: Add CORS headers on the server: Access-Control-Allow-Origin
Use a proxy in development (e.g., Vite/webpack proxy)
Ensure preflight OPTIONS requests are handled
